cards.description:
Plan: Decompose app.rs into Focused Modules

Context

crates/kanban-tui/src/app.rs is a 2,060-line god object with 58 public fields spanning unrelated concerns: mode navigation, focus tracking, entity selection, filtering, multi-select, dialog state, sprint views, relationships, animations, persistence, and UI state. All 9 handler files (impl App) freely mutate any field. This makes it impossible to discover where to add/change something without reading the entire file, and causes constant merge conflicts.

The goal is purely structural: split fields and their co-located logic into focused sub-structs in named modules with zero behavioral change. After this refactor, adding a new sprint feature means editing sprint_view.rs. Adding a filter means editing filter.rs. The App struct becomes a thin orchestrator.

Target Structure

crates/kanban-tui/src/app/
├── mod.rs          ← Thin App struct + new() + run() + handle_key_event() + execute_action()
├── mode.rs         ← AppMode, DialogMode, ModeManager (push/pop/open_dialog/is_dialog/get_base_mode)
├── focus.rs        ← Focus, CardFocus, BoardFocus, FocusState
├── selection.rs    ← SelectionHub (board/card/sprint selection + active indices)
├── filter.rs       ← FilterState (sprint filters, hide_assigned, sort field/order, search, filter_dialog)
├── multi_select.rs ← MultiSelectState (selected_cards, selection_mode_active)
├── dialog_input.rs ← DialogInputState (import, priority, column, sprint_assign, task_list_view selections)
├── sprint_view.rs  ← SprintTaskPanel enum, SprintViewState (panel + card lists + components)
├── relationship.rs ← RelationshipState (card_ids, selected, selection, search, parents/children lists)
├── view.rs         ← ViewState (strategy, card_list_component, viewport_height, last_frame_area)
├── animation.rs    ← CardAnimation, ANIMATION_DURATION_MS, AnimationState
├── persistence.rs  ← PersistenceState (save_file, app_config, file_watcher, channels)
└── ui_state.rs     ← UiState (banner, help_list, help_pending_action, card_navigation_history)

Migration Steps (each must compile before proceeding)

Step 1: app.rs → app/mod.rs (mkdir + mv). Files Touched: 0. Effort: 5 min
Step 2: Extract enums to mode.rs, re-export. Files Touched: mod.rs. Effort: 20 min
Step 3: Extract Focus/CardFocus/BoardFocus/SprintTaskPanel to focus.rs, sprint_view.rs. Files Touched: mod.rs. Effort: 15 min
Step 4: Extract CardAnimation + constant to animation.rs. Files Touched: mod.rs. Effort: 10 min
Step 5: Introduce ModeManager with Deref; mode+mode_stack → mode: ModeManager. Files Touched: mod.rs + ~20 write sites in handlers/ui.rs. Effort: 75 min
Step 6: Introduce FocusState; 3 fields → focus: FocusState. Files Touched: 9 handler files, ui.rs, render_strategy.rs, keybindings/registry.rs. Effort: 45 min
Step 7: Introduce SelectionHub; 5 fields → selection: SelectionHub. Files Touched: all 9 handlers, app.rs, ui.rs, state/snapshot.rs. Effort: 75 min
Step 8: Introduce FilterState; 7 fields → filter: FilterState. Files Touched: app.rs, navigation/sprint/card/filter_handlers, state/snapshot.rs. Effort: 40 min
Step 9: Introduce MultiSelectState; 2 fields → multi_select: MultiSelectState. Files Touched: navigation/popup/card_handlers, app.rs. Effort: 20 min
Step 10: Introduce DialogInputState; 6 fields → dialog_input: DialogInputState. Files Touched: popup/card/column/detail_view/board_handlers. Effort: 25 min
Step 11: Introduce SprintViewState; 5 fields → sprint_view: SprintViewState. Files Touched: app.rs, popup/card/detail_view_handlers, ui.rs. Effort: 35 min
Step 12: Introduce RelationshipState; 7 fields → relationship: RelationshipState. Files Touched: navigation/popup/card/detail_view_handlers. Effort: 30 min
Step 13: Introduce ViewState; 4 fields → view: ViewState. Files Touched: app.rs, navigation/card_handlers, ui.rs. Effort: 20 min
Step 14: Introduce AnimationState; 1 field → animation: AnimationState. Files Touched: app.rs, card_handlers. Effort: 20 min
Step 15: Introduce PersistenceState; 6 fields → persistence: PersistenceState. Files Touched: app.rs, state/mod.rs only. Effort: 20 min
Step 16: Introduce UiState; 4 fields → ui: UiState; keep App shims. Files Touched: app.rs, detail_view_handlers. Effort: 20 min
Step 17: Move method bodies into sub-struct impls (populate, apply_sort, sync, switch_strategy, animation tick). Files Touched: app.rs + sprint_view/view/animation modules. Effort: 45 min

Total: ~8 hours

Critical Files

- crates/kanban-tui/src/app.rs → becomes app/mod.rs
- crates/kanban-tui/src/ui.rs — updated in steps 6, 11, 13, 16
- crates/kanban-tui/src/state/snapshot.rs — apply_to_app accesses active_board_index + current_sort_field; updated in steps 7 and 8
- crates/kanban-tui/src/keybindings/registry.rs — reads app.mode, app.focus, app.card_focus, app.board_focus; updated in steps 5 and 6
- crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/detail_view_handlers.rs — touches the most sub-struct categories; use as integration canary

Verification

After each step: cargo check --package kanban-tui
After all steps: cargo build && cargo test --package kanban-tui && cargo clippy -- -D warnings
cards.title: Decompose app.rs into Focused Modules

cards.description: In settings_handlers.rs apply_storage_location_change(), file_existed is captured before async migration but used to branch logic after. A concurrent process could create/delete the file in between. Currently acceptable for a single-user app but worth tracking. File: crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/settings_handlers.rs lines 126-143.
cards.title: Fix TOCTOU in storage migration: file_existed check vs load

cards.description: In kanban-service/src/config.rs move_config(), the rename() fallback to copy+delete is non-atomic. If the process crashes between copy and remove_file, both old and new config files exist. The error IS surfaced if delete fails (so no silent duplication during normal operation), but a crash window exists. Consider adding a .tmp suffix during copy and renaming atomically.
cards.title: Document move_config cross-device fallback crash window

cards.description: kanban_bin() now searches CARGO_TARGET_DIR across all subdirs and profiles (release before debug) so nix sandbox builds with cross-compiled target triples are found without KANBAN_BIN injection. KANBAN_BIN override preserved.
cards.title: Fix kanban binary discovery in MCP integration tests for nix builds

cards.description: Navigation in settings hardcodes index skip logic (e.g. skip indices 5-6 when cli_file_override). Introduce a disabled/non-selectable flag on list component items so the navigation layer can skip disabled items generically instead of with bespoke index checks.
cards.title: Add disabled option support to list components to avoid hardcoded skip logic

cards.description:
## Context

`kanban-mcp` currently shells out to the `kanban` CLI binary for all operations (board/column/card/sprint CRUD). This creates two problems:

1. **Build cost**: Nix builds of `kanban-mcp` must also build the full `kanban-cli` binary as a runtime dep.
2. **Storage backend isolation**: When KAN-159 lands, the MCP server cannot participate in pluggable storage — it is locked into whatever store the CLI subprocess uses, with no injection point.

## Relationship to KAN-159

KAN-159 establishes `DynStore = Arc<dyn PersistenceStore + Send + Sync>` as the abstraction seam in the TUI. The same pattern needs to apply to `kanban-mcp`. Without this card, MCP is stranded on JSON forever even after SQLite ships.

## Goal

Extract `crates/kanban-cli/src/handlers/` into a new `kanban-service` crate whose functions accept `Arc<dyn PersistenceStore + Send + Sync>`. Both `kanban-cli` and `kanban-mcp` depend on `kanban-service` and wire up their own store instance.

## Target Dependency Graph

```
kanban-cli --+
             +--> kanban-service --> kanban-persistence (PersistenceStore trait)
kanban-mcp --+                            ^              ^
                                   JsonFileStore     SqliteStore
```

## Work Items

- [ ] Create crates/kanban-service/ with handlers migrated from kanban-cli/src/handlers/
- [ ] Handlers accept Arc<dyn PersistenceStore + Send + Sync> instead of reading disk directly
- [ ] Update kanban-cli to depend on kanban-service (no behavioral change)
- [ ] Rewrite kanban-mcp/src/context.rs to call kanban-service directly (replace executor.rs subprocess model)
- [ ] Remove kanban from crates/kanban-mcp/default.nix nativeBuildInputs and postInstall wrap
- [ ] Update kanban-mcp integration tests to not require KANBAN_BIN

## Acceptance Criteria

- kanban-mcp Nix derivation has no dependency on the kanban or kanban-cli binary
- MCP server works with both JSON and SQLite stores (after KAN-159 merges)
- No behavioral regression in CLI or MCP
cards.title: Extract kanban-service crate from kanban-cli handlers

cards.description: In PR #206, default_output_path now uses backend name as extension directly, so 'kanban migrate src.json sqlite' produces src.sqlite instead of src.db. Add a note to the changeset and consider whether to keep the new behavior or restore .db as the SQLite default extension.
cards.title: fix: migrate CLI default extension changed from .db to .sqlite (silent breaking change)

cards.description:
Submit the kanban CLI to the Arch User Repository so Arch users can install via `yay -S kanban`.

No approval needed — push and it goes live immediately.

## Prerequisites

- AUR account at https://aur.archlinux.org
- SSH key registered in AUR profile:
  ```
  ssh-keygen -f ~/.ssh/aur
  # add contents of ~/.ssh/aur.pub to AUR account > My Account > SSH keys
  ```
- Add to ~/.ssh/config:
  ```
  Host aur.archlinux.org
    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/aur
    User aur
  ```

## Package Name

Use `kanban` — not taken in official repos, `kanban-bin` is taken but for a different project (GitLab kanban). Plain source build gets the plain name.

## PKGBUILD

Create a `PKGBUILD` file:

```bash
# Maintainer: Your Name <email>
pkgname=kanban
pkgver=0.3.5
pkgrel=1
pkgdesc="Terminal-based kanban board with MCP server integration"
arch=("x86_64")
url="https://github.com/youruser/kanban"
license=("MIT")
makedepends=("rust" "cargo")
source=("$pkgname-$pkgver.tar.gz::https://github.com/youruser/kanban/archive/v$pkgver.tar.gz")
sha256sums=("SKIP")

build() {
    cd "$pkgname-$pkgver"
    cargo build --release --locked --bin kanban
}

package() {
    cd "$pkgname-$pkgver"
    install -Dm755 "target/release/kanban" "$pkgdir/usr/bin/kanban"
    install -Dm644 LICENSE "$pkgdir/usr/share/licenses/$pkgname/LICENSE"
}
```

Replace sha256sums SKIP with the real hash once the release tarball exists:
```
curl -L <tarball-url> | sha256sum
```

## Submission

```bash
git clone ssh://aur@aur.archlinux.org/kanban.git
cd kanban
cp /path/to/PKGBUILD .
makepkg --printsrcinfo > .SRCINFO
git add PKGBUILD .SRCINFO
git commit -m "Initial import"
git push
```

## Ongoing Maintenance

- Update pkgver + sha256sums on each release, regenerate .SRCINFO, push
- Watch AUR comments for bug reports
- If you stop maintaining, disown via the AUR web UI


AUR auto-publish workflow
---

 After each GitHub release of kanban, the AUR package at aur.archlinux.org/kanban needs to be updated with the new version and sha256. This should be fully automated via a separate GitHub Actions workflow — decoupled from the release job so a failure doesn't block a
 release.

 No shared scripts, no flake inputs between repos. Just a standalone workflow with ~20 lines of shell.

 Files to create

 .github/workflows/aur-publish.yml (new, in main kanban repo)

 Triggered by release: published. Clones the AUR repo, updates PKGBUILD and .SRCINFO, commits and pushes.

```yaml
 name: Publish to AUR

 on:
   release:
     types: [published]

 jobs:
   aur-publish:
     name: Update AUR package
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
     steps:
       - uses: cachix/install-nix-action@v27
         with:
           github_access_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
           extra_nix_config: |
             experimental-features = nix-command flakes

       - name: Setup AUR SSH key
         run: |
           mkdir -p ~/.ssh
           echo "${{ secrets.AUR_SSH_KEY }}" > ~/.ssh/aur_key
           chmod 600 ~/.ssh/aur_key
           ssh-keyscan aur.archlinux.org >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts

       - name: Clone AUR repo
         env:
           GIT_SSH_COMMAND: "ssh -i ~/.ssh/aur_key -o IdentitiesOnly=yes"
         run: |
           git clone ssh://aur@aur.archlinux.org/kanban.git /tmp/aur-kanban

       - name: Update PKGBUILD and .SRCINFO
         run: |
           VERSION="${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}"
           VERSION="${VERSION#v}"
           TARBALL_URL="https://github.com/fulsomenko/kanban/archive/v${VERSION}.tar.gz"
           SHA256=$(curl -sL "$TARBALL_URL" | sha256sum | awk '{print $1}')

           cd /tmp/aur-kanban
           sed -i "s/^pkgver=.*/pkgver=${VERSION}/" PKGBUILD
           sed -i "s/^pkgrel=.*/pkgrel=1/" PKGBUILD
           sed -i "s/sha256sums=.*/sha256sums=(\"${SHA256}\")/" PKGBUILD

           MAKEPKG_CONF=$(find /nix/store -name "makepkg.conf" -path "*/pacman-*/etc/*" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
           nix-shell -p pacman libarchive --run \
             "makepkg --printsrcinfo --config ${MAKEPKG_CONF}" 2>/dev/null \
             | grep -v "^Kanban\|^📦\|^🦀" > .SRCINFO

       - name: Commit and push
         env:
           GIT_SSH_COMMAND: "ssh -i ~/.ssh/aur_key -o IdentitiesOnly=yes"
         run: |
           cd /tmp/aur-kanban
           git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
           git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
           git add PKGBUILD .SRCINFO
           git commit -m "Update to ${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}"
           git push
```

 Secret setup

 1. Generate key

 ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f /path/to/key -C "github-actions kanban AUR" -N ""

 2. Store in sops-nix

 Add the private key to your sops secrets and activate it via NixOS. Path managed by sops.

 3. Register public key on AUR

 1. cat /path/to/key.pub
 2. https://aur.archlinux.org → My Account → SSH Public Key → paste and save

 4. Add to GitHub Actions secrets

 gh secret set AUR_SSH_KEY --repo fulsomenko/kanban < <(sops -d path/to/encrypted/aur-key)

 Required secret name: AUR_SSH_KEY

 Verification

 After merging and setting the secret:
 1. Publish a release (or re-publish an existing one) to trigger the workflow
 2. Pull ~/fulsomenko/aur/kanban and check for a new commit
 3. Verify AUR page reflects the version
cards.title: Submit kanban to AUR

cards.description: Unlike CliContext::load and McpContext::new, validate_and_load_store does not call sync_backend_with_file before opening the store. A backend/file mismatch produces a confusing error. Either apply sync there too, or add a doc comment making the caller's responsibility explicit.
cards.title: fix: validate_and_load_store skips sync_backend_with_file unlike CLI/MCP entry points

cards.description: In config.rs::move_config, when fs::rename fails and the fallback copy+delete path is taken, the result of remove_file(new_path) is ignored with let _ = .... Add a tracing::warn\! so failures during cleanup are observable without propagating the error.
cards.title: fix: move_config silently drops cleanup error on partial failure

cards.description:
`aggregate-changelog.sh` produces a flat list of every commit message, losing the
per-feature grouping that older entries (≤ 0.1.10) had. The 0.3.0 entry has 60+
raw commit lines instead of sections grouped by ticket/changeset.

**Current output (0.3.0):**
```
- feat: update MCP server tools for full CLI parity
- feat: bring MCP context to full parity with CLI
- fix: error handling in MCP executor
...
```

**Expected output (matching 0.1.10 style):**
```
### KAN-193 Bring MCP to full feature parity (2026-03-17)
- feat: update MCP server tools for full CLI parity
- feat: bring MCP context to full parity with CLI
...

### KAN-212 Paginated list responses (2026-03-17)
- feat(core): add PaginatedList<T> ...
```

## Cause

`aggregate-changelog.sh` reads changeset files (one per ticket) but concatenates
their descriptions into a single flat section rather than emitting a heading per
changeset.

## Acceptance criteria

- Each changeset file produces its own `###` heading in the CHANGELOG, using the
ticket ID and description from the changeset frontmatter
- Commit-level bullet points appear under their respective heading
- The 0.3.0 entry is retroactively corrected to match the grouped format
- Future releases produce grouped output automatically
cards.title: CHANGELOG.md grouping by card

cards.description: test_app_config_dto_serializes_keys_in_alphabetical_order checks JSON key order by parsing lines, but serde_json does not guarantee struct field serialization order. The test may break across serde versions. Replace with BTreeMap or serde_json::Value comparison, or remove the ordering assertion.
cards.title: fix: AppConfigDto key-order test relies on undefined serde_json field ordering

cards.description: The current bulk-update pattern (bulk-archive, bulk-move) feels like an afterthought. A more natural API would be `kanban cards update` (plural) supporting heterogeneous field updates across multiple cards in one command, e.g. different points per card. This mirrors how users think: 'update cards' rather than 'card bulk-update'.
cards.title: Add 'kanban cards update' plural subcommand for bulk heterogeneous updates

cards.description: Regression introduced in commit 88862dc. Root cause: SprintViewState::default() always initialises panel = Uncompleted, and populate_sprint_task_lists also resets it to Uncompleted. The h/l panel-switch keys only work when sprint.status == Completed. So for any Active or Planning sprint the panel is permanently Uncompleted, making the else { handle_complete_sprint_key() } branch unreachable. Fix: restore original c key logic. Multi-select (via v) is explicit, so c with no multi-select unambiguously means complete sprint. Files: crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/detail_view_handlers.rs
cards.title: fix: c key can no longer complete sprint from sprint detail view

cards.description: using the `p` dialog to set the points for a card doesnt apply the value to the card
cards.title: `p` dialog does not correctly set points

cards.description: Added 'Attachments, adding files to cards' as the first planned item in the Roadmap section of web/index.html to match README.md.
cards.title: Sync web/index.html roadmap with README.md

cards.description: the same seems to apply for actions across the board
cards.title: dont reset search on scroll

cards.description:
It would be cool to emulate the vim search binding with `escape` to clear search, `enter` to apply search and `n` to go to next hit. ofcourse with highlighting of the search string on the hit element

like I hit c to complete the task and it took me away from the search results. search should only clear explicitly by hitting escape

currently. searching and then hitting escape escape the user out of the board

also actions taken after the search has been made clears the search. I would like to keep the search results until the users explictly exits search mode
cards.title: escape bind to clear search, enter to apply

cards.description: settings_handlers.rs in kanban-tui exceeds the 300-line module limit (599 lines). Split into handlers/settings/{mod.rs, config_edit.rs, storage.rs, export.rs} to align with project conventions. Deferred from PR #206 review.
cards.title: refactor: split settings_handlers.rs (599 lines) into focused sub-modules

cards.description:
find_card_by_identifier uses .find() and returns the first match, which is ambiguous when two cards on different boards share the same card number and prefix (e.g. two boards both using KAN prefix both having card #210).

## Problem

CardIdentifierSearcher::matches() checks prefix+number but has no board scope. If the caller does not filter by board first, the wrong card can be returned — as demonstrated when 'KAN-210' resolved to an older card instead of the newly created one.

## Options

- Add an optional board_id scope to find_card_by_identifier so callers can narrow the search
- In CLI/MCP contexts where a single board is in use, auto-scope to that board
- Return all matches and error if ambiguous (forcing the caller to use a UUID)
- Document the limitation and require UUID when identifier is ambiguous
cards.title: Disambiguate card identifier lookup across boards

cards.description:
Currently undo/redo stores full Snapshot clones (all boards, columns, cards, sprints, graph) for every operation. This is wasteful — editing a single card title clones the entire board state.

Design and implement a command-based undo mechanism where each command produces an inverse operation (e.g., UpdateCard stores the old field values, CreateCard produces DeleteCard). This enables:
- Per-field undo granularity for external editor edits
- Significantly reduced memory usage for undo history
- Composable batch undo (a bulk operation stores N inverse commands as one entry)

Scope:
- Define an InverseCommand trait or extend Command with fn inverse() -> Box<dyn Command>
- Migrate existing commands to produce inverses
- Update HistoryManager to store inverse commands instead of Snapshots
- Update KanbanContext.undo()/redo() to execute inverses
- Ensure external editor mutations (board name, card title, card metadata JSON) use per-field inverses
- Maintain backward compatibility: snapshot-based undo can coexist during migration

Depends on: KAN-264/KAN-265 (history-aware execute in KanbanContext)
cards.title: Command-based undo: per-field inverse operations instead of full snapshots

cards.description: Currently search is limited (KAN-236, KAN-237 cover partial groundwork). Need a first-class search experience: full-text search across card titles, descriptions, and tags; filtering by multiple fields simultaneously (priority, status, assignee, points range, due date); search in both TUI (fuzzy, incremental) and CLI/MCP (structured query). Should replace the scattered filter/search workarounds currently spread across the codebase.
cards.title: Proper search functionality across cards, boards, and columns

cards.description: Avoid vec![Box::new(cmd) as Box<dyn Command>] at every single-command call site
cards.title: Add execute_one(Box<dyn Command>) convenience method to KanbanContext

cards.description: Add gitignore rules for *.sqlite3, *-wal, *-shm, and *-journal files. These are generated at runtime by the SQLite persistence backend (WAL mode) and should not be tracked.
cards.title: Add *.sqlite3 and SQLite auxiliary files to .gitignore

cards.description:
## Goal

Replace the single flat `KanbanError` enum (in `kanban-core`) with per-layer error types that have precise, minimal responsibility. This is the foundation for all future error handling across the codebase.

## Current Problem

`KanbanError` in `kanban-core` is used by every crate, meaning:
- Persistence concepts (`ConflictDetected`, `Io`) sit next to domain concepts (`CycleDetected`, `NotFound`)
- The core crate couples upward to higher-layer concerns
- Callers cannot match precisely — `NotFound(String)` loses type information
- Graph errors (`CycleDetected`, `SelfReference`, `EdgeNotFound`) are domain-specific but in core

## Target Architecture

```
kanban-core        → KanbanError (thin top-level wrapper, used by service + CLI + TUI + MCP)
kanban-domain      → DomainError (NotFound, Validation, DependencyError)
kanban-persistence → PersistenceError (Io, Serialization, ConflictDetected)
```

Each layer only knows its own error type. Boundaries convert via `#[from]`.

## Layer Design

### kanban-domain: DomainError

```rust
#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum DomainError {
    #[error("{entity} {id} not found")]
    NotFound { entity: &'static str, id: Uuid },

    #[error("validation error: {0}")]
    Validation(String),

    #[error(transparent)]
    Dependency(#[from] DependencyError),
}

#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum DependencyError {
    #[error("cycle detected: adding this edge would create a circular dependency")]
    CycleDetected,

    #[error("self-reference not allowed")]
    SelfReference,

    #[error("edge not found")]
    EdgeNotFound,
}
```

`NotFound { entity: &'static str, id: Uuid }` — typed id, readable message, no enum bloat as domain grows.

Helper constructors on `DomainError` keep call sites clean:
```rust
impl DomainError {
    pub fn board_not_found(id: Uuid) -> Self { Self::NotFound { entity: "board", id } }
    pub fn card_not_found(id: Uuid)  -> Self { Self::NotFound { entity: "card",  id } }
    pub fn column_not_found(id: Uuid)-> Self { Self::NotFound { entity: "column",id } }
    pub fn sprint_not_found(id: Uuid)-> Self { Self::NotFound { entity: "sprint",id } }
    pub fn archived_card_not_found(id: Uuid) -> Self { Self::NotFound { entity: "archived card", id } }
}
```

### kanban-persistence: PersistenceError

```rust
#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum PersistenceError {
    #[error("IO error: {0}")]
    Io(#[from] std::io::Error),

    #[error("serialization error: {0}")]
    Serialization(String),

    #[error("file conflict: {path} was modified by another instance")]
    ConflictDetected {
        path: String,
        #[source]
        source: Option<Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync>>,
    },
}
```

### kanban-core: KanbanError (top-level wrapper)

```rust
#[derive(Error, Debug)]
pub enum KanbanError {
    #[error(transparent)]
    Domain(#[from] DomainError),

    #[error(transparent)]
    Persistence(#[from] PersistenceError),

    #[error("internal error: {0}")]
    Internal(String),
}
```

TUI, CLI, and MCP work with `KanbanError`. They can match on `KanbanError::Domain(DomainError::NotFound { entity, id })` when they need precision, or treat all errors uniformly for display.

## Migration Steps

1. **Add `DomainError` + `DependencyError` to `kanban-domain`**
   - New file: `crates/kanban-domain/src/error.rs`
   - Export from `crates/kanban-domain/src/lib.rs`

2. **Add `PersistenceError` to `kanban-persistence`**
   - New file: `crates/kanban-persistence/src/error.rs`
   - Export from `crates/kanban-persistence/src/lib.rs`

3. **Update `kanban-core` KanbanError**
   - Depends on `kanban-domain` and `kanban-persistence` (check if circular — may need to move KanbanError out of core entirely, or keep core as the aggregator crate)
   - Remove `NotFound`, `Validation`, `Io`, `Serialization`, `ConflictDetected`, `CycleDetected`, `SelfReference`, `EdgeNotFound`, `Connection`

4. **Update `kanban-domain` command and graph code**
   - All commands return `DomainResult<()>` (type alias for `Result<T, DomainError>`)
   - Graph errors return `DependencyError`

5. **Update `kanban-persistence`**
   - All persistence code returns `PersistenceResult<T>`

6. **Update `kanban-service`**
   - Service methods return `KanbanResult<T>` as today
   - `?` operator handles conversion via `#[from]`

7. **Update TUI, CLI, MCP**
   - Match arms updated where needed
   - `cargo check` drives this mechanically

## Watch Out For

- `kanban-core` currently has no dependencies on other workspace crates — if `KanbanError` wraps `DomainError`, core would need to depend on domain, inverting the dependency flow. Consider making `kanban-service` own the top-level error instead, with `kanban-core` remaining a pure abstractions crate (traits + result type only).
- `KanbanResult<T>` type alias must be updated at its definition site

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `DomainError` defined in `kanban-domain` with `NotFound { entity, id }`, `Validation`, `Dependency` variants
- [ ] `DependencyError` sub-enum with `CycleDetected`, `SelfReference`, `EdgeNotFound`
- [ ] `PersistenceError` defined in `kanban-persistence`
- [ ] `KanbanError` in core (or service) wraps both via `#[from]`
- [ ] No stringly-typed `NotFound(String)` anywhere in the codebase
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` passes
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace` passes clean
- [ ] KAN-171 can use `DomainError::card_not_found(id)` directly in CommandContext helpers
cards.title: Per-layer error types: DomainError, PersistenceError, KanbanError

cards.description:
## Problem

The `sqlite` feature in `kanban-cli` is opt-in, meaning neither `default.nix` nor `nixpkgs/package.nix` ship with SQLite storage support. Users who open a `.db`/`.sqlite`/`.sqlite3` file get no backend match.

## Changes Required

### 1. `crates/kanban-cli/Cargo.toml`
Add `sqlite` to the default features:
```toml
default = ["tui", "sqlite"]
```

### 2. `crates/kanban-service/Cargo.toml`
Add `sqlite-storage` to the default features:
```toml
default = ["json-storage", "sqlite-storage"]
```

### 3. `default.nix`
No changes needed — it already builds with default features (unless `withTui` is false, in which case `--no-default-features` is passed and SQLite would also be excluded, which is fine for the MCP-only use case).

### 4. `nixpkgs package.nix`
No changes needed — plain `buildRustPackage` picks up defaults. The sqlx sqlite feature bundles its own SQLite (no extra `nativeBuildInputs` required).

## Build Implications

| Concern | Impact |
|---|---|
| Compile time | sqlx + bundled SQLite C compilation adds time to clean builds (~30-60s). Incremental builds unaffected. |
| Binary size | ~1-2 MB increase from statically linked SQLite engine. |
| System deps | None — sqlx 0.8 bundles SQLite by default, no system library needed. |
| Cross-platform | SQLite C code compiles everywhere Rust does, `platforms = all` stays valid. |
| Runtime | Zero cost if user never opens a SQLite file — factory just registers in StoreRegistry. |

## Affected Crates
- `kanban-cli` (feature gate)
- `kanban-service` (feature gate)
- `kanban-tui` (already has `sqlite` feature, will be activated transitively via `kanban-cli`)
cards.title: Make SQLite a default feature in kanban-cli

cards.description: In apply_config_edit, the inline path resolution (is_absolute / current_dir().join) duplicates logic from resolve_storage_location. Extract a private is_same_location(loc: &str, resolved: &str) -> bool helper that delegates to resolve_storage_location to eliminate the duplication and the hidden dependency on from_config serialising absolute paths.
cards.title: Extract strip_storage path resolution into helper

cards.description:
## Context

KAN-256 fixed SQLite `.db` loading by adding a second async load path in `run()`, but introduced some issues worth cleaning up.

## Issues to Address

1. **Extension allowlist duplicates `SqliteStoreFactory::matches`** — hardcoded `"db" | "sqlite" | "sqlite3"` in `App::new()` must stay in sync with the factory. Unify by always deferring to `store.load()` when `state_manager.store().is_some()`, removing the extension check entirely.

2. **JSON files get loaded twice** — once via `import_board_from_file` in `new()`, then again via `store.load()` in `run()` (since `JsonStoreFactory` also creates a store). The store load wins but the first is wasted work with double migration.

3. **Restore removed comment** — `// Clear undo/redo history after initial file load (not an undoable action)` was removed without reason.

4. **No test for async store load path** — add an integration test that creates a SQLite store, saves data, and verifies it loads correctly on startup.

## Proposed Fix

Make `store.load()` in `run()` the **only** initial load path when a store exists. Remove `import_board_from_file` from `new()` entirely. Adjust `test_failed_import_clears_save_file` to test through the async path.
cards.title: Unify initial file loading path (follow-up to KAN-256)

cards.description: test_open_config_editor_skips_apply_when_content_matches only validates the trim() comparison in isolation — it does not assert that apply_config_edit is actually not called. Replace with an integration-level assertion that verifies the absence of side effects (e.g. config file not written, migration_state stays Idle) when the editor returns unchanged content.
cards.title: Strengthen open_config_editor no-op guard test

cards.description:
The current `migrate` CLI is awkward: `--from` specifies the source file and `--to` specifies an output file whose extension determines the target backend type. 

Instead, make the target backend a positional parameter and the filename an argument to the migrate command. 

This is more explicit and less surprising than inferring the backend from the file extension.
cards.title: Rework migrate CLI: backend as positional arg, filename as option

cards.description: Replace O(n²) nested iteration in ImportEntities with HashSets built once before the loop
cards.title: Optimize ImportEntities duplicate check: pre-build HashSets outside loop

cards.description:
## Goal
Track sprint velocity and burndown data so completed work can be reviewed after a sprint closes.

## Scope
- Add `committed_points` and `completed_points` to the Sprint model
- Capture a `BurndownEntry { date, remaining_points, completed_points }` whenever a card status changes within an active sprint
- Calculate velocity on sprint completion and store it on the sprint record
- Add a migration for existing sprints (fields default to null/empty)

## Out of scope
TUI visualization (charts, burndown graph) — that is a separate card.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sprint stores committed vs completed points at completion time
- [ ] Burndown entries are appended on each card status change during an active sprint
- [ ] Velocity is calculated and persisted when a sprint is completed
- [ ] Existing sprints load without error after migration
- [ ] Unit tests for velocity calculation and burndown accumulation
cards.title: Sprint velocity and burndown tracking
Extract ui.rs into reusable components and view submodules

cards.description:
## Summary

Implemented a full-screen Settings view accessible via `S` from the Boards panel.

## What was added

### kanban-core
- `AppConfig` gained two new optional fields: `default_db_mode` and `default_format` (with `effective_*` accessors defaulting to `"json"`)
- `save()` / `save_to(path)` / `load_from(path)` methods for config persistence
- `AppConfigDto` implementing `Editable<AppConfig>` for JSON editor support
- `CoreError::Config` variant
- 7 unit tests

### kanban-tui
- `AppMode::Settings` variant
- `KeybindingAction::OpenSettings`
- `SettingsViewProvider` keybinding provider (`e` to edit, `q/Esc` to go back)
- `S` key bound in Normal mode (both hardcoded dispatch and registry)
- `settings_handlers.rs`: `handle_open_settings()` (only from Boards focus) and `handle_settings_key()` (edit via `edit_entity_json_impl`, save, escape)
- `render_settings_view()` with three sections:
  - **Configuration** (branch prefix, default DB mode, default format) — editable via `e`
  - **Storage** (file path, backend type, instance ID) — read-only
  - **Config File** (path, loaded/not found status) — read-only
- 6 integration tests

### Files changed
- `crates/kanban-core/src/config.rs` — new fields, save/load, DTO, tests
- `crates/kanban-core/src/error.rs` — Config variant
- `crates/kanban-core/src/lib.rs` — export AppConfigDto
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/error.rs` — From<CoreError> arm
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mode.rs` — Settings variant
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs` — key dispatch + action wiring
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/keybindings/mod.rs` — OpenSettings action + pub mod
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/keybindings/settings.rs` — **new**
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/keybindings/normal_mode.rs` — S binding
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/keybindings/registry.rs` — Settings provider
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/mod.rs` — pub mod
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/settings_handlers.rs` — **new**
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/ui.rs` — render_settings_view + wiring
- `crates/kanban-tui/tests/settings_tests.rs` — **new**
cards.title: Settings page UI

cards.description: From PR #213 review. The execute() API now takes Vec<Box<dyn Command>>, which requires wrapping every single-command call site with vec\![Box::new(cmd) as Box<dyn Command>]. There are ~100 such sites in kanban-tui alone. Consider a cmd\!() macro or a single() helper fn: fn cmd(c: impl Command + 'static) -> Vec<Box<dyn Command>> { vec\![Box::new(c)] }. Low priority cleanup.
cards.title: Add helper to reduce vec\![Box::new(...) as Box<dyn Command>] boilerplate

cards.description: Both annotate_storage_fields and comment_storage_fields use Vec::join("\n") which silently drops any trailing newline from the input. Most text tools expect a trailing newline; stripping it on round-trip could cause spurious diffs. Also: the hardcoded 2-space indent on the JSON comment will misalign if the file uses a different indent width.
cards.title: Fix trailing newline drop in annotate/comment_storage_fields

cards.description:
## Context
Current `JsonFileStore` loads entire state into memory and serializes full JSON on every save. This is O(n) for both read and write, blocking scalability past ~1000 cards.

## Architecture: Implement Behind PersistenceStore Trait
The `PersistenceStore` trait in `kanban-persistence/src/traits.rs` is the seam for adding new storage backends.

## Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Schema Design
Create `schema.sql` with tables for: metadata, boards, columns, cards, sprints, archived_cards. Add indexes for common queries (cards by column, cards by sprint).

### Phase 2: SqliteStore Implementation
New file: `kanban-persistence/src/store/sqlite_store.rs`
- Use `sqlx` with SQLite driver (async-compatible)
- Implement `PersistenceStore` trait
- Transaction wrapping for atomic operations
- Connection pooling for concurrent access

### Phase 3: Incremental Saves (Key Optimization)
Instead of full-state snapshots:
- Track dirty entities in `DirtyTracker` struct
- Only INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE changed rows
- Use `updated_at` timestamps for conflict detection

### Phase 4: Migration from JSON
- Detect `.json` vs `.db` file extension
- Auto-migrate: load JSON -> create SQLite -> write all entities
- Keep JSON as backup until verified

## SOLID Considerations
- **Single Responsibility**: SqliteStore only handles persistence, not domain logic
- **Open/Closed**: Add new store without modifying existing code
- **Liskov**: SqliteStore substitutable for JsonFileStore via trait
- **Dependency Inversion**: TUI/CLI depend on PersistenceStore trait, not concrete impl

## Abstraction for JSON vs SQL
Create shared `StoreOperations` trait for common patterns that both JsonFileStore and SqliteStore implement.

## Files to Create/Modify
- `crates/kanban-persistence/Cargo.toml` - add sqlx, feature flag
- `crates/kanban-persistence/src/store/sqlite_store.rs` - new
- `crates/kanban-persistence/src/store/mod.rs` - export SqliteStore
- `crates/kanban-persistence/src/schema.sql` - DDL
- `crates/kanban-persistence/src/migration/json_to_sqlite.rs` - new
- `crates/kanban-cli/src/main.rs` - store selection logic

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SqliteStore implements PersistenceStore trait
- [ ] Feature flag: `--features sqlite` to enable
- [ ] JSON->SQLite migration preserves all data
- [ ] Incremental saves (not full snapshots)
- [ ] Concurrent read safety via connection pool
- [ ] Benchmarks showing improvement over JSON
cards.title: Implement SQLite storage backend

cards.description: Confirmed bug from PR #213 review. The mutating_op! macro in crates/kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs calls reload() after every mutation to refresh state, but does not call clear_history() afterward. This causes undo/redo history to accumulate across reloads in MCP — the TUI file watcher path correctly calls clear_history() after reload(), but MCP does not. Fix: add ctx.clear_history() call inside mutating_op! after the reload() call.
cards.title: Fix: MCP mutating_op! calls reload() without clear_history()

cards.description: TUI calls Sprint::assignable() from the domain layer directly in 5+ places (card_handlers.rs, popup_handlers.rs, selection_dialog.rs, app/mod.rs). This bypasses the service layer, which is the pattern the rest of the codebase follows after PR #213. Fix: add KanbanContext::list_assignable_sprints(board_id: Uuid) -> Vec<Sprint> delegating to Sprint::assignable(), then update all TUI call sites to go through the service.
cards.title: Expose list_assignable_sprints on KanbanContext
Lift undo/redo (HistoryManager) into KanbanContext

cards.description: From PR #213 review. KanbanContext now owns the store (moved from StateManager). TuiContext exposes replace_store() for backend migration paths. Verify: (1) all code paths that trigger a backend switch go through replace_store(), (2) no path can swap the backend on KanbanContext directly without going through TuiContext, (3) channels are reset correctly via reset_save_channels() in all migration flows.
cards.title: Audit replace_store() call sites on TuiContext for backend migration correctness

cards.description:
## Context
Domain layer uses command pattern (`kanban-domain/src/commands/`), but commands are not persisted. Need to evaluate if this adds value or just complexity.

## Current Implementation Analysis

### What Exists
- `Command` trait with `execute()` and `description()`
- Commands for all CRUD operations
- `CommandContext` with mutable refs to all state
- Command descriptions logged to `VecDeque<String>` (audit only)

### What's Missing for Event Sourcing
- Commands not persisted to storage
- No replay mechanism
- No event versioning
- No projection rebuilding
- No undo/redo

### Questions to Answer
1. Are commands persisted? -> No, only snapshots
2. Is this event-sourcing? -> No, just structural pattern
3. Can we implement undo/redo? -> Not without persisting commands
4. Does the abstraction help? -> Provides uniform API, but adds indirection

## Evaluation Criteria
- Keep if: Command pattern enables future undo/redo, improves testability
- Simplify if: Just adding boilerplate without benefit

## Recommendation
Keep command pattern BUT document its purpose:
- Enables uniform operation interface for TUI/CLI
- Provides audit log via descriptions
- Future-proofs for undo/redo if commands are persisted
- Add `Undoable` trait for commands that support reversal

## Files to Analyze
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/mod.rs`
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/*.rs` - all command impls
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/state/mod.rs` - command execution

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Document command pattern purpose in code comments
- [ ] Add `Undoable` trait (empty for now, marks intent)
- [ ] Create `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` explaining design decisions
- [ ] Decision: keep, simplify, or extend for undo/redo
cards.title: Evaluate command pattern implementation

cards.description:
## Problem

`next_sprint_number` on `Board` is stored state that has drifted out of use:
- `allocate_sprint_number()` (the only method that reads/writes it) is **never called** anywhere in the codebase
- Sprint numbers are actually assigned via `sprint_counters: HashMap<String, u32>` (keyed by prefix) through `get_next_sprint_number(prefix: &str)` in `board.rs`

`sprint_counters` has the same fundamental problem — it is stored state that can diverge from reality when the file is hand-edited or migrated. `ensure_sprint_counter_initialized()` already derives the correct starting value from existing sprints (max sprint_number for the prefix + 1), proving the information is fully derivable.

This caused a concrete bug: `demo/fixtures/demo.json` ended up with two sprints both labelled KAN 1 because `sprint_counters` fell out of sync during manual editing.

## Options

1. **Remove `next_sprint_number` only** — dead field cleanup. Low risk, small change.
2. **Remove both `next_sprint_number` and `sprint_counters`** — replace with a computed function `next_sprint_number_for(prefix, sprints) -> u32` that returns `max(sprint.sprint_number where sprint.prefix == prefix) + 1`, defaulting to 1. Eliminates the entire class of drift bugs.

Option 2 is preferred. The max-scan cost is negligible at this scale.

## Affected files

- `crates/kanban-domain/src/board.rs` — remove field + `allocate_sprint_number()`, replace `get_next_sprint_number()` with pure function
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/sprint_commands.rs` — pass sprints slice to the new function instead of mutating board counter
- `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/upserts.rs` — drop `next_sprint_number` column writes
- `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/builders.rs` — drop `next_sprint_number` field read
- `crates/kanban-persistence-json/` — field will naturally stop being serialized once removed from struct
- Tests in `kanban-service`, `kanban-persistence-sqlite` that reference `next_sprint_number`
- `demo/fixtures/demo.json` — fix the duplicate KAN 1 sprint_number while touching this
cards.title: Remove next_sprint_number and derive sprint counters from sprints
Warn when storage falls back to implicit CWD kanban.json
Improve demo workflow

cards.description: Currently only the data file is watched (FileWatcher). config.toml edits from outside the TUI (e.g. text editor) are not picked up until restart. Question: is config file watching worth implementing? Storage location changes would need full persistence re-init; display/theme settings could hot-reload more easily. In-app editor already triggers reload — gap is external edits only.
cards.title: Investigate: watch config.toml for external edits while TUI is running

cards.description:
Two bugs caused ~/.config/kanban/config.toml to be written when it shouldn't be and to become corrupted when it was, plus related fixes for CLI-supplied storage paths and the settings UI.

Fix 1 — Atomic config write (kanban-service):
Replaced truncate+write with NamedTempFile+rename in write_config_file. On Unix sets 0o600 permissions before writing. Prevents partial writes on crash (the 'ix"' corruption pattern). Promoted tempfile to a regular dependency.

Fix 2 — Skip save when editor exits without changes (kanban-tui):
Added content-equality guard in open_config_editor so quitting with :q (no changes) is a no-op — no config file written.

Fix 3 — CLI-supplied storage path is session-only (kanban-tui):
strip storage fields from the DTO when cli_file_override is true, restore CLI path after saving, skip apply_storage_location_change.

Fix 4 — Absolute storage path not injected when CLI arg matches config default (kanban-tui):
App::new no longer writes the resolved absolute path into app_config.storage_location when the CLI arg resolves to the same file as the configured default.

Fix 5 — Unchanged storage not written to config (kanban-tui):
apply_config_edit compares DTO storage location against the resolved active path; strips it if unchanged.

Fix 6 — CLI override unloadable via config editor:
If the user uncomments storage fields in the editor while cli_file_override is active, the override is dropped and new storage settings are persisted permanently.

Settings UI — Storage label routing:
Added cli_file_provided flag. Configuration panel shows three layouts:
- Config-only: Storage Backend / Storage Location (from config file)
- CLI-only: Active Storage Backend / Active Storage Location (from CLI arg)
- CLI overrides config: both rows, config values greyed out
After migration or override drop, cli_file_provided resets so labels revert to Storage *.
Storage Location values now always show resolved absolute paths.
cards.title: Fix config file corruption and unnecessary writes
Hide grayed config storage rows when storage is not set in config

cards.description:
## Context

Discussed in CAT-260 session. Two related but distinct capabilities worth designing before adding more backends.

---

## Use Case 1: Board routing (per-board store selection)

Different boards live in different backends:

```
work_board    → work.sqlite
personal      → ~/notes/personal.json
archive_2024  → archive://2024.tar.gz
```

Requires a routing layer: board_id → store locator. Currently absent from the architecture. The StoreRegistry can hold many factories but each invocation resolves to exactly one store instance.

---

## Use Case 2: Entity federation (intra-board splitting)

Within a single board, different entity types live in different stores:

```
board metadata  → config.json
cards           → cards.sqlite        (queryable, indexed)
attachments     → ~/docs/             (markdown files, one per card)
activity log    → append-only.log
```

---

## Key design insight

A FederatedStore could itself implement PersistenceStore. It assembles a Snapshot by reading from multiple sub-stores and writes back to the right one per entity type. From KanbanContext perspective it is still one store — federation is opaque to the rest of the system. This fits the existing plugin pattern: FederatedStoreFactory is just another factory that instantiates multiple sub-stores internally.

```
KanbanContext
  └── FederatedStore  (implements PersistenceStore)
        ├── SqliteStore     ← cards, columns
        ├── JsonStore       ← board metadata, config
        └── MarkdownStore   ← card descriptions as .md files
```

---

## Open design questions

- **Consistency**: if writing cards to sqlite succeeds but writing markdown fails, what is the state? Need a compensation or rollback strategy.
- **Assembly**: what is the merge key when loading? board ID? card ID?
- **Config shape**: how does a user express 'cards for board X go to Y, attachments go to Z'? Needs a BoardLocator or routing config format.
- **Migration**: how do you move an entity from one sub-store to another without data loss?
- **Cross-store operations**: moving a card between boards in different stores requires coordinating two stores with no transaction guarantee between them.

---

## Relationship to backend consolidation (CAT-260)

The current one-to-one (app → store) architecture is fine for now. This is a future direction. Decide the shape of BoardLocator and FederatedStore before adding more backends so the config format does not need to change later.
cards.title: Multi-store architecture: board routing and entity federation

## What was done

- Fixed inaccuracies: removed 'due date' from sort options (not implemented), removed story points range (1–5)

### Crate READMEs

- kanban-domain: fixed field name card_counters → prefix_counters

### Web Landing Page (web/)
- New hero: value headline, install command as primary CTA, secondary nav links
- Added Why Kanban? section with differentiator bullets

- Reordered: Features before Installation

### Demo Infrastructure
- Packaged agentstation/vhs fork (v0.11.1) in demo/vhs.nix — adds SVG output support
- Added devShells.demo to root flake (imports demo/shell.nix with flake pkgs)

- Set Framerate 16 for natural playback speed

### Tracked separately

cards.title: README, web landing page, and demo infrastructure revamp

cards.description: Currently KanbanContext loads the entire dataset into Vec<T> fields on startup. PersistenceStore is a snapshot interface (load/save blobs). This makes storage backends interchangeable serialization formats only — SQLite's query engine goes unused. Goal: PersistenceStore becomes a query interface (list_boards, list_cards(filter), get_card, upsert_card, delete_card, etc.). KanbanContext becomes a thin coordinator that delegates reads/writes to the store. SQLite then runs real WHERE clauses. JSON backend becomes honest: loads everything, queries in memory. Undo/redo needs rethinking without full-world clones.
cards.title: Refactor storage to on-demand querying instead of full snapshot in memory

cards.description: The early-return path in run_with_args for the Completions subcommand is untested. Add a test: run_with_args(["kanban", "completions", "bash"]) on CliApp::default() should return Ok(()).
cards.title: Add test for Completions subcommand early-return path

cards.description: CliApp::with_defaults() manually registers SqliteStoreFactory/JsonStoreFactory, duplicating the logic already in kanban_service::default_registry(). McpServer::with_defaults() does this correctly — CliApp should too.
cards.title: CliApp::with_defaults() should delegate to default_registry()

## Documentation Revamp — Root README + 9 Crate READMEs

Complete rewrite of all project documentation to be accurate, dense, and compelling.

### Files Modified

| File | Lines | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|
| `README.md` | 373 | Full rewrite: hero tagline, feature grid, installation, quick start, full keybinding tables for all 5 contexts, architecture diagram + crate table, persistence details, roadmap |
| `crates/kanban-mcp/README.md` | 182 | All 40 tools documented (was 14); organized by section with parameter types |
| `crates/kanban-domain/README.md` | 307 | All models fully documented: Board, Column, Card (every field), Sprint, SprintLog, ArchivedCard, FieldUpdate, DependencyGraph, HistoryManager, error hierarchy, business rules |
| `crates/kanban-tui/README.md` | 321 | New: module structure, AppMode/DialogMode enums (all 26 dialogs), focus system, view strategies, event loop, full keybinding tables, editor integration, clipboard, components |
| `crates/kanban-service/README.md` | 236 | Expanded: all KanbanContext fields, complete method tables for all operation groups, undo/redo API, state accessors, utility functions, command execution flow |
| `crates/kanban-cli/README.md` | 180 | New: full command reference for board/column/card/sprint/top-level, card identifier resolution, JSON output format, BatchOperationResult, shell completions, env vars |
| `crates/kanban-core/README.md` | 174 | Fixed phantom traits (removed Repository/Service/Loggable that don't exist); documented AppConfig, PaginatedList, Page/PageInfo, InputState, SelectionState, Editable, Graph |
| `crates/kanban-persistence/README.md` | 159 | New: PersistenceStore, StoreFactory, StoreRegistry, ChangeDetector, Serializer, MigrationStrategy, all shared types |
| `crates/kanban-persistence-json/README.md` | 101 | New: V2 envelope format, save/load flows, atomic writes, conflict detection, debounced saving, JsonStoreFactory match logic |
| `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/README.md` | 112 | New: SqliteStore, connection pool config, all 14 tables, transactional save/load, schema versioning, SqliteStoreFactory magic-byte detection |

### Key Fixes
- MCP README previously listed 14 tools; now documents all 40
- kanban-core previously referenced phantom `Repository`, `Service`, `Loggable` traits not present in source
- kanban-tui and kanban-cli had no READMEs
- Card model was missing `sprint_logs`, `completed_at`, `assigned_prefix`, `card_prefix` field docs
- Status transition semantics (Done <-> completed_at) were undocumented
- All keybinding tables verified against `crates/kanban-tui/src/keybindings/` source
cards.title: Revamp all documentation — root README + 9 crate READMEs

cards.description: pub store_manager in kanban-tui app/mod.rs is wider than needed — only accessed within the same crate by settings_handlers.rs. Change to pub(crate).
cards.title: app/mod.rs: store_manager should be pub(crate) not pub

cards.description:
The release workflow continued to the tag/GitHub release steps even after publish-crates.sh exited with code 1. Add safeguards so a partial publish causes the entire job to abort and surface a clear failure, preventing tag/version mismatches and orphaned crates on crates.io.

## Resolution

Already implemented. publish-crates.sh has `set -uo pipefail` and the publish loop explicitly calls `exit 1` on failure (lines 43-45). The CI job will abort on any crate publish failure.

No code change needed.
cards.title: Fail release job immediately if any crate publish fails

cards.description:
## Problem

The carry-over dialog exists but is not reachable from the natural sprint completion flow. Three separate gaps:

---

### Gap 1: Auto-trigger silently skipped when no Planning sprint exists

**File:** `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/sprint_handlers.rs` lines 135-150

When completing a sprint, the dialog only appears if a Planning sprint already exists. If there is none, the dialog is skipped entirely with no feedback. The user has no idea carry-over was even possible.

**Fix:** After sprint completion, if there are uncompleted cards but no Planning sprint, show a message:
```
"Sprint completed. X cards not carried over (no planning sprint available)."
```
Or offer to create a new Planning sprint inline.

---

### Gap 2: 'c' key silently does nothing on a completed sprint

**File:** `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/sprint_handlers.rs` lines 88-106

`handle_complete_sprint_key()` returns `None` silently when the sprint is already Completed or Cancelled. The 'c' keybinding description still says "Complete this sprint" regardless of sprint status.

**Fix options:**
- Change keybinding hint text to "Carry over cards" when viewing a completed sprint
- Or show an error/info message: "Sprint already completed. Use M to carry over cards."

---

### Gap 3: 'M' key for carry-over is undiscoverable

**File:** `crates/kanban-tui/src/keybindings/sprint_detail.rs` lines 109-114
**File:** `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/detail_view_handlers.rs` lines 705-714

'M' is the only way to trigger carry-over from a completed sprint view, but it is not shown in the context help bar when the sprint is completed. Users have no way to know it exists.

**Fix:** Ensure 'M' ("Carry over cards") appears in the bottom help bar when viewing a Completed or Cancelled sprint.

---

### Summary of expected UX

| User action | Current behaviour | Expected behaviour |
|---|---|---|
| Press 'c' to complete sprint with uncompleted cards + Planning sprint exists | Dialog shown | (correct) |
| Press 'c' to complete sprint with uncompleted cards + no Planning sprint | Silent skip | Show message / offer to create Planning sprint |
| View completed sprint, press 'c' | Silent no-op | Show hint to use 'M' or rebind |
| View completed sprint, press 'M' | Works but undiscoverable | Show in help bar |
cards.title: KAN-210 follow-up: carry-over dialog disconnect from sprint completion flow

cards.description: Both Card::branch_name() and CardIdentifierSearcher::get_identifier() manually duplicate the sprint.card_prefix → board.card_prefix → "task" chain instead of calling Sprint::effective_card_prefix(). They're functionally equivalent today but will silently diverge if the resolution logic changes. Refactor both to use the shared method.
cards.title: Unify card identifier prefix resolution via Sprint::effective_card_prefix()

cards.description:
Goal: open_context() establishes a connection with zero I/O. Data is loaded lazily on first DataStore/CommandStore call. Eliminates the is_sqlite fork across all three apps (TUI, CLI, MCP).

# Challenge

KanbanContext::load() today: (1) calls PersistenceStore::load() fetching full snapshot blob, (2) deserializes everything into InMemoryStore, (3) loads command log. This is eager-load-everything. All three apps fork on is_sqlite because JSON and SQLite have different initialization contracts.

# TARGET ARCHITECTURE

DataStore + CommandStore are the sole runtime interface. PersistenceStore (blob) is used only for import/export/migration. KanbanBackend gains lifecycle methods: flush(), reload(), needs_flush(), needs_save_worker(), on_undo_state_changed(), instance_id().

### STEP 1 — KanbanBackend lifecycle methods [DONE]

Add flush/reload/needs_flush/needs_save_worker/on_undo_state_changed/instance_id to KanbanBackend with default no-op impls. SQLite impl: flush()=WAL checkpoint, reload()=no-op, needs_flush()=false. InMemoryStore uses all defaults.
Files: crates/kanban-service/src/backend.rs, crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/sqlite_store.rs, crates/kanban-domain/src/data_store.rs

### STEP 2 — JsonDataStore lazy JSON backend [DONE]

New type in crates/kanban-service/src/json_backend.rs wrapping Arc<dyn PersistenceStore> + RwLock<Option<InMemoryStore>> + AtomicBool dirty. Construction is zero-I/O. ensure_loaded() reads file on first access using block_in_place (requires multi_thread runtime). flush() serialises cache to disk. reload() sets inner=None so next access re-reads. needs_save_worker()=true (background flush worker needed). on_undo_state_changed() caches cursor+baseline for next flush.

### STEP 3 — Simplify KanbanContext [DONE]

Single constructor KanbanContext::open(backend, config) — zero I/O. baseline_snapshot: Option<Snapshot> starts None, captured lazily on first execute(). command_count restored lazily on first undo/redo via ensure_undo_state_initialized(). save() delegates to backend.flush().await. reload() delegates to backend.reload().await and clears baseline_snapshot. Removed store: Option<Arc<dyn PersistenceStore>> field, replace_store(), store() getter.
File: crates/kanban-service/src/context.rs

### STEP 4 — StoreManager::make_backend() [DONE]

New method returning Arc<dyn KanbanBackend>. If is_sqlite → SqliteStore::open(). Else → JsonDataStore::new(make_store()). Also make_backend_sync() for sync contexts (uses block_on internally).
File: crates/kanban-service/src/store_manager.rs

### STEP 5 — kanban_service::open_context() [DONE]

Single public entry point: sync_backend_with_file() + make_backend() + KanbanContext::open(). All three apps can call this instead of duplicating the StoreManager dance.
File: crates/kanban-service/src/lib.rs

### STEP 6 — Wire FileWatcher to backend.reload() [DONE]

auto_reload_from_external_change() in app/mod.rs now calls ctx.reload() → backend.reload() instead of store.load().await directly. SaveCoordinator sends () flush signals instead of Snapshot blobs; save worker calls backend.flush().await on each signal. TuiContext uses needs_save_worker() to decide whether to spawn the background worker.
Files: crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs, crates/kanban-tui/src/state/mod.rs, crates/kanban-tui/src/tui_context.rs, crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/settings_handlers.rs

### STEP 7 — Unify App Initialization [PENDING]

Replace divergent is_sqlite forks in all three apps with open_context().

TUI: Collapse App::new_with_store() + App::open_sqlite() into App::new(ctx: KanbanContext). Remove TuiContext::from_context(); TuiContext::new(ctx) is the only constructor. SaveCoordinator::new() uses ctx.backend().needs_flush() instead of store.is_some().

CLI: Remove if is_sqlite { App::open_sqlite() } else { App::new_with_store() } fork in app.rs. CliContext::load() calls kanban_service::open_context() directly. Remove StoreManager parameter.

MCP: McpContext::new() calls kanban_service::open_context() directly. Remove StoreManager parameter.

Delete dead code: App::open_sqlite(), App::new_with_store(), TuiContext::from_context(), resolve_is_sqlite() helper.

TDD tests to write first:

- test_tui_context_single_constructor_json (save_rx is Some)
- test_tui_context_single_constructor_sqlite (save_rx is None)
- test_cli_init_and_run_json_unified_path
- test_cli_init_and_run_sqlite_unified_path
- test_mcp_list_boards_json_unified_path
- test_mcp_list_boards_sqlite_unified_path

Files to modify:

- crates/kanban-cli/src/context.rs
- crates/kanban-mcp/src/context.rs
- crates/kanban-mcp/src/server.rs
- crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs
- crates/kanban-tui/src/tui_context.rs

VERIFICATION (all steps)

1. cargo test --workspace — all tests pass
2. cargo run -- init --name Test test.json && cargo run test.json — JSON path works
3. cargo run -- init --name Test test.sqlite && cargo run test.sqlite — SQLite path works
4. Modify test.json externally while TUI open → conflict banner appears
5. Modify test.sqlite externally → next read reflects new state transparently
6. MCP tool calls persist for both backends
7. Undo/redo works on both backends after lazy baseline change

---

 TuiContext::new(ctx) is the only constructor. SaveCoordinator::new() uses ctx.backend().needs_save_worker() instead of

VERIFICATION (all steps)
