cards.description:
## Why

KAN-394 made the status ↔ completion-column auto-sync project-wide:
single-card and batch `update_card`/`move_card`/`update_cards`/`move_cards`
all chain commands to keep `CardStatus::Done` ⇔ "card lives in the
resolved completion column". That behaviour is opinionated. Some
workflows treat the last column as a regular stage (e.g. "Done by dev,
awaiting review") rather than a completion gate, and want
`status=Done` set without the card being yanked into that column —
or to move a card into the last column without it being marked
complete.

Today the auto-sync is unconditional. There is no way to opt out.

## What

Add a per-board configuration knob. Suggested shape:

```rust
pub struct Board {
    // ... existing fields
    pub auto_sync_completion: bool,  // defaults to true
}
```

When `false`:
- `KanbanContext::update_card(_, CardUpdate { status: Some(Done), ..)` sets the
  status without chaining a `MoveCard` to the completion column.
- `KanbanContext::move_card(_, completion_col_id, _)` moves the card without
  chaining `status=Done`.
- `update_cards` and `move_cards` (batch) follow the same rule.
- Atomic updates that pin both `column_id` and `status` are unaffected
  (caller intent always wins).

## Where the wiring lives

- `crates/kanban-domain/src/board.rs` — add the field, surface it on
  `BoardUpdate` via `FieldUpdate<bool>`, migrate JSON V2 → V3 (default
  true for existing boards so behaviour stays the same on upgrade).
- `crates/kanban-service/src/context.rs` — the four service methods
  (`update_card`, `move_card`, `update_cards`, `move_cards`) each
  resolve the board once and skip the chained command when the flag is
  off. The pure helpers in `kanban_domain::card_lifecycle` stay
  unchanged — they remain "what would happen if auto-sync fired"; the
  service decides whether to call them.
- CLI: surface a flag on `kanban board update` (`--auto-sync-completion <bool>`).
- MCP: expose on `tool_update_board`.
- TUI: settings page / board configuration dialog.

## Acceptance

- New board defaults to auto-sync = on (backwards compatible).
- Existing boards on disk read as auto-sync = on after migration.
- Per-board toggle is honoured by every entry point — there's a test
  per surface (service, MCP integration, CLI integration, TUI handler).
- `crates/kanban-service/tests/completion_sync.rs` gains an
  `auto_sync_off` variant of each existing test.

## Notes / Out of scope

- This is a per-board flag, not a per-card override. Cards inherit from
  their board.
- A potential follow-up: app-level default for `auto_sync_completion`
  applied to new boards (probably overkill — board-level is enough).
- Touches no domain commands; they remain pure single-responsibility.
cards.title: feat(service): make status ↔ completion-column auto-sync configurable per board

cards.description:
## Concept

The command log lives in-memory and is session-scoped (KAN-405). For users who want cross-session undo without sacrificing a clean data file, we can borrow nvim's approach: a separate, optional undo file that stores the command log independently from the data layer. The data file stays clean and human-readable; the undo file is an ephemeral sidecar that the user can opt into, delete freely, and version-control separately or ignore entirely.

## How nvim does it

nvim writes a .un~ file alongside each edited file. It stores a tree of undo/redo branches. The file is silently ignored if missing or corrupted. Kanban should follow the same contract: the undo file is opt-in, best-effort, and never required for correct operation.

## Proposed design

### File location and naming

- Default: same directory as the data file with a suffix, e.g. board.json -> board.json.undo
- Configurable via AppConfig: undo_dir = ~/.local/share/kanban/undo/ with filenames derived from the data file path hash
- If the undo file cannot be read or is malformed, silently discard it and start with a clean slate

### File format

JSON Lines or a simple binary envelope (baseline snapshot + ordered command batches):

{
  format_version: 1,
  data_file_hash: sha256 of the data file path (for validation),
  session_id: uuid of the session that wrote this file,
  baseline: <Snapshot as JSON>,
  commands: [ [batch0_cmd0, ...], [batch1_cmd0, ...], ... ]
}

The baseline is the snapshot at the time the undo file was started. Commands are the ordered batches applied after that baseline. This is exactly the in-memory state of KanbanContext serialised to disk — so loading it is a straightforward deserialise and populate.

### UndoStore trait (new, in kanban-persistence or kanban-service)

pub trait UndoStore: Send + Sync {
    fn load(&self) -> KanbanResult<Option<UndoState>>;
    fn save(&self, state: &UndoState) -> KanbanResult<()>;
    fn clear(&self) -> KanbanResult<()>;
}

pub struct UndoState {
    pub baseline: Snapshot,
    pub commands: Vec<Vec<Command>>,
    pub cursor: usize,
}

Implementations:
- NullUndoStore: no-op (default, current behaviour)
- FileUndoStore: reads/writes the sidecar file described above

### Integration in KanbanContext

On open: if an UndoStore is configured, call load(). If it returns Some(state), populate baseline_snapshot, command log, and undo_cursor from it. If it returns None or errors, fall back to a clean session.

On execute/undo/redo: after any state change, call save() asynchronously (debounced, same as the main save worker). Errors are logged but not fatal.

On session close / clear_history: call clear() to delete the undo file so it does not accumulate stale state.

### Activation

Opt-in via config:

[undo]
enabled = true          # default: false
dir = ~/.local/share/kanban/undo   # optional, defaults to data file directory

Or via CLI flag: kanban --undo-file board.json.undo

### Safety properties

- The undo file is never required for data integrity. Deleting it is always safe.
- If the data file changes between sessions (e.g. external edit, migration), the baseline hash check fails and the undo file is discarded.
- The undo file is not committed to version control (add *.undo to .gitignore in project templates).
- Undo file writes use atomic rename, same as the main JSON file.

## Relationship to other cards

- KAN-405: established that the command log must not be in the data file. This card is the opt-in escape hatch for users who want cross-session undo without polluting kanban.json.
- KAN-410: message-based bus for cross-client undo — complementary, not competing. Bus = live sync; undo file = crash recovery and single-user cross-session undo.
cards.title: Design: separate undo file for optional cross-session command log persistence

cards.description:
## Why

After KAN-394 moved the status ↔ completion-column orchestration from
the TUI handlers up into the service layer (`KanbanContext::update_card`,
`move_card`, `update_cards`, `move_cards`), several pure helpers in
`kanban_domain::card_lifecycle` lost all their production callers.
They remain in the codebase only to support their own inline unit tests.

Verified at HEAD with:

```
grep -rn "compute_completion_toggle" crates/ | grep -v "fn compute_completion_toggle\|card_lifecycle.rs"
# (empty — zero external callers)

grep -rn "\.new_status" crates/kanban-tui/src/ crates/kanban-service/src/ \
                       crates/kanban-cli/src/ crates/kanban-mcp/src/
# (empty — zero readers of CardMoveResult.new_status)

grep -rn "CompletionToggleResult" crates/ | grep -v card_lifecycle
# (empty — type is module-private in practice)
```

Carrying dead code creates two ongoing costs:

1. The inline unit tests give a false sense that the helper is
   exercised — but no production path runs through it.
2. The `new_status` field on `CardMoveResult` is a footgun: a future
   caller might read it expecting the lifecycle sync, not realise the
   service layer already chains the same logic, and end up double-syncing.

## What

Delete the dead surface from `crates/kanban-domain/src/card_lifecycle.rs`:

- `pub fn compute_completion_toggle(...) -> Option<CompletionToggleResult>`
- `pub struct CompletionToggleResult { new_status, target_column_id, new_position }`
- The `new_status: Option<CardStatus>` field on `pub struct CardMoveResult`
  (keep `target_column_id` and `new_position` — those *are* used)
- The inline tests under `// --- compute_completion_toggle ---` and the
  status-flip-related assertions inside the `compute_card_column_move` tests
- Update `compute_card_column_move` to return only `target_column_id` and
  `new_position` — the status sync is no longer this function's responsibility

Keep:

- `target_column_for_status` and `target_status_for_column_move` — both
  used by `KanbanContext::compute_target_column_for_status` and
  `KanbanContext::compute_target_status_for_move`
- `compute_card_column_move` itself — still used by
  `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs::handle_move_card` and
  `move_selected_cards` to resolve left/right direction → target column
- `sorted_board_columns`, `next_position_in_column`,
  `compact_column_positions`, `should_auto_complete_new_card`,
  `resolve_restore_column`, `migrate_sprint_logs`

## Where the wiring lives

- `crates/kanban-domain/src/card_lifecycle.rs` — delete the items above,
  shrink `CardMoveResult` to two fields, update its docstring.
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs` (lines ~425, ~502) —
  no code change needed: both call sites already ignore `result.new_status`
  after KAN-394's refactor. Confirm with `cargo build` after the field drop.
- Update the module-level doc comment at the top of `card_lifecycle.rs`
  if it references the deleted items.

## Acceptance

- `compute_completion_toggle` / `CompletionToggleResult` symbols are
  fully gone from the codebase (`rg compute_completion_toggle` returns
  zero hits, ditto `CompletionToggleResult`).
- `CardMoveResult` has exactly two fields: `target_column_id`,
  `new_position`.
- `cargo build --workspace`, `cargo test --workspace`,
  `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and
  `cargo fmt --check` all clean.
- No new test cases needed (we're deleting tests, not adding behaviour);
  the existing `crates/kanban-service/tests/completion_sync.rs` and
  `crates/kanban-tui/tests/batch_completion_sync_tests.rs` already lock
  in the actual lifecycle behaviour.

## Commit shape

Single `chore(domain): drop dead lifecycle helpers superseded by KAN-394`
commit. Roughly:

- ~60 lines removed in `card_lifecycle.rs` (function body + struct +
  inline tests + the `new_status` computation inside
  `compute_card_column_move`)
- No production callers touched
- Test count drops by ~6 inline tests; service + tui integration tests
  unchanged

## Notes / Out of scope

- This does **not** touch `target_column_for_status` or
  `target_status_for_column_move` — those are the live helpers the
  service layer now depends on.
- This does **not** revisit the auto-sync semantics — that's a separate
  follow-up (configurability: KAN-432).
- If a future contributor wants the toggle-completion shape back, they
  should reach for the service-layer method directly (`update_card`
  with `status` set), not reintroduce the helper.
cards.title: chore(domain): drop dead lifecycle helpers superseded by KAN-394

cards.description:
Follow-up from PR #247 (Amanda Frost's Windows duplicate-keypress fix).

The fix in crates/kanban-tui/src/events.rs filters non-Press KeyEventKind variants on Windows. It was merged without tests because the EventHandler path polls crossterm directly, which makes isolated testing awkward.

Scope:
- Extract the filtering predicate into a small pure function (e.g. should_accept_key(&KeyEvent) -> bool) so it can be unit-tested without a real event loop.
- Add unit tests covering: Press accepted on all platforms; Repeat/Release dropped on Windows; Repeat/Release accepted on Linux (cfg-gated tests).

Note: the question of whether `Repeat` should be accepted (held-key behavior under Windows Terminal VT-input mode) is tracked separately in KAN-413. Pick the predicate behavior that matches the resolution of that ticket — if KAN-413 is still open when this is picked up, mirror the current filter exactly so tests lock in present behavior.
cards.title: Add tests for Windows KeyEventKind::Press filter in events.rs

cards.description:
## Why

After KAN-434 collapsed `update_card` and `assign_card_to_sprint` into
shorthands over their plural counterparts, code review surfaced an
inefficiency in the underlying pattern: three batch service methods on
`KanbanContext` compute their `KanbanResult<usize>` return value by
**reading the backend twice** (before-the-mutation count and
after-the-mutation count), then returning the delta.

The three offenders, all in `crates/kanban-service/src/context.rs`:

```
:1048  fn archive_cards            — list_archived_cards() × 2
:1057  fn move_cards               — list_cards_by_column(column_id) × 2
:1145  fn assign_cards_to_sprint   — list_cards_by_sprint(sprint_id) × 2
```

The before/after pattern is the only honest way to compute "how many
cards were actually changed" given today's command surface — some of
the input IDs may already be on the sprint / already archived /
already in the target column (no-ops), and some may be invalid (the
domain commands' `filter_valid_card_ids` helper silently skips them).
Counting the delta on the backend is correct, but each of those
`list_*` calls is wasted work that the underlying domain command
already knows the answer to.

There's a second cost surfaced specifically by KAN-434's collapse:
the singular wrappers (`assign_card_to_sprint`, and the pre-existing
`archive_card`) call the plural to get the `usize`, discard it, then
fetch the card. So for the single-card path the two reads are pure
overhead — the singular doesn't even use the count.

On in-memory and JSON backends the cost is microseconds. On SQLite
it's two additional indexed queries per call. Negligible per
operation; becomes measurable at high frequency or large boards.

## What

Push the mutation count down into the domain command's execute path
so the service can return it directly without re-reading the backend.

### Domain-layer change (the load-bearing part)

In `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/card_commands.rs`, change the
three offending commands' `execute` from `KanbanResult<()>` to
`KanbanResult<usize>`:

- `ArchiveCards::execute`         (line ~254)
- `MoveCards::execute`            (line ~294)
- `AssignCardsToSprint::execute`  (line ~336)

Each command already knows which IDs it actually mutated (it calls
`filter_valid_card_ids` and iterates them). Returning that count is a
local change inside each `execute` body — `Ok(valid_ids.len())`
typically.

### CardCommand enum dispatch

`crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/card_commands.rs:27` —
`CardCommand::execute` currently returns `KanbanResult<()>`. It needs
to either (a) also become `KanbanResult<usize>` with non-batch arms
returning `Ok(0)` or `Ok(1)`, or (b) keep its `()` return and add a
separate `executed_count` method per command. Option (a) is more
honest — every mutation is "n entities affected" — but it touches the
command-log replay path. Option (b) keeps the existing replay code
path untouched.

**Recommendation: Option (a).** It generalises naturally: `CreateCard`
returns 1, `DeleteCard` returns 1, `UpdateCard` returns 1, etc. Both
options are tracked here so the implementer can pick.

### Service-layer cleanup

In `crates/kanban-service/src/context.rs::execute(Vec<Command>)`
(line 158), propagate the per-command count out so callers can sum it.
Then rewrite the three plurals:

```rust
fn archive_cards(&mut self, ids: Vec<Uuid>) -> KanbanResult<usize> {
    let counts = self.execute(vec![Command::Card(CardCommand::Archive(ArchiveCards { ids }))])?;
    Ok(counts.into_iter().sum())  // exactly one command, one count
}

fn move_cards(&mut self, ids: Vec<Uuid>, column_id: Uuid) -> KanbanResult<usize> {
    let chained_status_updates = self.chained_status_updates_for_batch_move(&ids, column_id)?;
    let mut batch = vec![Command::Card(CardCommand::MoveMultiple(MoveCards { ids, column_id }))];
    for (card_id, new_status) in chained_status_updates {
        batch.push(/* chained UpdateCard */);
    }
    let counts = self.execute(batch)?;
    Ok(counts[0])  // first command is the MoveCards batch; status flips are not "moves"
}

fn assign_cards_to_sprint(&mut self, ids: Vec<Uuid>, sprint_id: Uuid) -> KanbanResult<usize> {
    let counts = self.execute(vec![Command::Card(CardCommand::AssignToSprint(
        AssignCardsToSprint { ids, sprint_id }
    ))])?;
    Ok(counts.into_iter().sum())
}
```

The `KanbanContext::execute` signature changes from
`fn execute(&mut self, commands: Vec<Command>) -> KanbanResult<()>`
to
`fn execute(&mut self, commands: Vec<Command>) -> KanbanResult<Vec<usize>>`.

That return shape lets the caller pick whichever index they care about
(the first command, the sum, etc.) without forcing every caller to
care about the count.

### `archive_card` and the singulars naturally cheapen

After this change, `assign_card_to_sprint` (a shorthand over
`assign_cards_to_sprint` post-KAN-434) and `archive_card` (a shorthand
over `archive_cards`) no longer pay for the wasted reads. The count
they discard was the wasted work; removing the source of the count
removes the waste.

## Where the wiring lives

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/card_commands.rs` | `ArchiveCards::execute`, `MoveCards::execute`, `AssignCardsToSprint::execute` return `KanbanResult<usize>`. Same change propagates to the `CardCommand::execute` dispatch arm depending on Option (a) vs (b). |
| `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/mod.rs` | If Option (a): `Command::execute` returns `KanbanResult<usize>` from its match. |
| `crates/kanban-service/src/context.rs` | `execute(Vec<Command>)` returns `KanbanResult<Vec<usize>>` (or `KanbanResult<usize>` if collapsed at the service boundary — caller's choice). Plurals at `:1048`, `:1057`, `:1145` use the returned counts instead of before/after delta queries. |

The command-log replay machinery on the persistence side
(`kanban-persistence-*` crates) shouldn't care about the count —
replay applies commands for their side effects, not their return
values. Confirm by `grep`ping for callers of `Command::execute` /
`CardCommand::execute` outside `KanbanContext` to make sure the
signature change doesn't break them.

## Reuse — already in place

- `filter_valid_card_ids` at
  `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/mod.rs::CommandContext` — every
  affected command already calls this to compute the actually-mutated
  ID set. The count is literally `valid_ids.len()` at the point each
  command finishes its body.
- `KanbanContext::execute` infrastructure — atomic batch, rollback,
  undo cursor. Unchanged in behaviour; only the return type widens.

## Acceptance

- The three plurals (`archive_cards`, `move_cards`,
  `assign_cards_to_sprint`) no longer call `list_*` for the count.
- Existing tests in `crates/kanban-service/tests/`,
  `crates/kanban-mcp/tests/`, and the contract-test helpers continue
  to pass without modification — the count semantics are preserved.
- Add a microbench or instrumented log assertion (optional) showing
  that a single-card `assign_card_to_sprint` makes one backend
  mutation call and zero count-queries.
- `cargo test --workspace`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`,
  and `cargo fmt --check` all clean.

## Behaviour change for existing callers

**None.** The return value (`usize`, count of cards actually mutated)
is identical to what the before/after delta produced. Pure
performance refactor.

## Risk

Medium. Touches the `Command::execute` signature, which is a
load-bearing trait used by:

- `KanbanContext::execute` (the atomic batch executor)
- The undo/redo replay path
- Any future cross-client command-log work

The change is mechanical but wide. Recommended approach:

1. Start with Option (b) on a feature branch: add per-command
   `executed_count()` methods alongside the existing `()` returns.
   This is additive — no existing caller breaks.
2. Switch the three service plurals to use the new method.
3. Verify all tests + the undo/redo path still work.
4. **Then** consider whether to converge on Option (a) (uniform
   `KanbanResult<usize>` from every `execute`) as a second step.

Doing Option (a) directly is shorter overall but harder to bisect if
something breaks.

## Notes / Out of scope

- This card does **not** address the `move_card` collapse blocker
  from KAN-434 (the `position: Option<i32>` mismatch with `move_cards`).
  That's a separate concern.
- This card does **not** introduce per-card success/failure detail —
  the `BatchOperationResult` returned by `*_detailed` inherent methods
  on `KanbanContext` already covers that case. Those methods have
  their own pattern and are out of scope unless the implementer
  decides to unify.
- The `update_cards` plural (post-KAN-394) does NOT have this issue —
  it returns `count = updates.len()` upfront because each input maps
  one-to-one to an `UpdateCard` command. Don't touch it.

## Relationship to other cards

- **KAN-434** (just merged) is the reason this gap is visible — the
  singular shorthands discard the count and pay for two extra reads
  per call. Resolving this card eliminates that overhead.
- **KAN-432** (per-board auto-sync opt-out) is independent. Both
  changes touch `update_cards` but in different ways.
- **KAN-433** (drop dead lifecycle helpers) is independent.

## Commit shape

If Option (b) (additive):

1. `feat(domain): card batch commands expose executed_count()` — add
   the new method to `ArchiveCards`, `MoveCards`,
   `AssignCardsToSprint`. Default to `0` for non-batch commands.
2. `refactor(service): replace before/after delta with command
   executed_count()` — switch the three plurals.
3. `chore: add changeset for KAN-43x`

If Option (a) (signature change):

1. `refactor(domain): Command::execute returns KanbanResult<usize>` —
   wide mechanical change.
2. `refactor(service): plurals use returned counts instead of
   before/after delta` — switch the three plurals.
3. `chore: add changeset for KAN-43x`

Either way: write the test commit first when introducing the new
return shape, per the project TDD convention.
cards.title: refactor(domain): commands return mutation count to remove before/after delta queries in service plurals

cards.description:
Follow-up from PR #247 (Amanda Frost's Windows duplicate-keypress fix).

The fix in crates/kanban-tui/src/events.rs filters with `key.kind != KeyEventKind::Press` on Windows, which also drops `KeyEventKind::Repeat` events. Amanda confirmed via manual testing that holding a key still repeats — the legacy Windows console typically emits multiple `Press` events at the OS level rather than `Repeat`, so this is fine today.

However, newer Windows Terminal can run with VT-input mode enabled, in which crossterm may emit `KeyEventKind::Repeat` events for held keys. Under the current filter those would be silently dropped, breaking key-repeat for Windows Terminal users with VT input on.

Scope:
- Reproduce: enable VT input mode in Windows Terminal and verify whether crossterm emits Repeat events for held keys.
- If yes: change the filter to `key.kind == KeyEventKind::Release` (drop only Release), or equivalently `matches!(key.kind, KeyEventKind::Press | KeyEventKind::Repeat)`.
- If the behavior is configuration-dependent, document the trade-off and pick the safer default.

References:
- https://github.com/veeso/tui-realm/issues/54
- https://github.com/ratatui/ratatui/issues/347
- https://docs.rs/crossterm/latest/crossterm/event/struct.KeyEvent.html
cards.title: Investigate KeyEventKind::Repeat handling on Windows Terminal VT-input mode

cards.description:
## Behaviour

In ColumnView and GroupedByColumn views, pressing `h` or `l` to move a
card across columns leaves the selector on whatever card was previously
selected in the **target** column, instead of following the moved card.
Equivalently, multi-selecting cards and pressing `h`/`l` to move them
leaves the selector in the wrong place.

## Expected

After `h`/`l`, the selector follows the moved card into its new column.
For the multi-select case, the selector follows the *first* selected
card (matching the existing implementation intent at
`crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs:535` —
`first_card_id` is computed precisely for this purpose).

## Why it happens

Same root cause as KAN-403, just at the move-card handlers:

- `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs:404::handle_move_card`
  ends with `self.select_card_by_id(card_id);` at line 480.
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs::move_selected_cards`
  ends with `self.select_card_by_id(card_id);` at line 536.

By the time `select_card_by_id` runs:

1. `self.ctx.move_card(card_id, target_column_id, None)` has updated the
   model — the card lives in `target_column_id` now.
2. The column-selection navigation block (lines 487–514) has shifted
   `self.dialog_input.column_selection` to the target column in
   ColumnView, so the *active task list* is now the target column's.
3. But the target column's `CardList::cards` vec is the cached one from
   the previous `prepare_frame()` — it does NOT yet contain the moved
   card's ID.
4. `select_card_by_id` → `task_list.select_card(card_id)` searches
   `self.cards`, doesn't find it, returns `false` silently.
5. The next render-loop `prepare_frame()` refreshes the task list and
   `CardList::update_cards` re-applies the saved `current_card` (the
   previously selected card in the target column). The moved card is
   ignored.

In **Flat** view this bug doesn't manifest — the active task list is
global and the card is in it before and after the move.

## Reproduction

```rust
// Add to crates/kanban-tui/tests/stale_model_regression_tests.rs
#[test]
fn test_move_card_right_selects_moved_card_in_kanban_view() {
    let mut app = App::test_default();
    let board = app.ctx.create_board("Board".to_string(), None).unwrap();
    let _todo = app.ctx.create_column(board.id, "Todo".to_string(), Some(0)).unwrap();
    let _doing = app.ctx.create_column(board.id, "Doing".to_string(), Some(1)).unwrap();
    let _done = app.ctx.create_column(board.id, "Done".to_string(), Some(2)).unwrap();
    app.prepare_frame();
    app.selection.board.set(Some(0));
    app.selection.active_board_index = Some(0);
    app.focus.active = Focus::Cards;

    // Two cards in Todo so there's a "prior selection" in Doing's
    // post-move task list to clobber the moved card's selection.
    app.input.set("Anchor".to_string());
    app.create_card();
    app.prepare_frame();
    app.input.set("Mover".to_string());
    app.create_card();
    app.prepare_frame();
    let mover_id = app.get_selected_card_id().unwrap();

    // Move "Mover" right: Todo → Doing.
    app.handle_move_card_right();
    app.prepare_frame();

    let selected = app.get_selected_card_id().expect("a card is selected");
    assert_eq!(
        selected, mover_id,
        "selector must follow the moved card into the target column, not stay on a prior card"
    );
}
```

A symmetric test should exist for `move_selected_cards` (multi-select
+ `l`).

## Fix — option 1: mirror KAN-403 per-handler

Insert `self.prepare_frame()` right before each `select_card_by_id`
call at the two sites above. One-line change per call site, two total.
Mirrors the fix landed for `create_card` in
[KAN-403 PR](https://github.com/fulsomenko/kanban/pull/260).

```rust
// handle_move_card, around line 480:
self.prepare_frame();
self.select_card_by_id(card_id);

// move_selected_cards, around line 536:
if let Some(card_id) = first_card_id {
    self.prepare_frame();
    self.select_card_by_id(card_id);
}
```

## Fix — option 2: centralize in `select_card_by_id` (preferred)

See companion card (filed alongside this one) for refactoring
`crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs:1454::select_card_by_id` to refresh
the task list internally. That eliminates every per-handler workaround,
including the one already in `create_card`. Both this card's bug and
KAN-403's are subsumed.

If the companion card lands first, this card is automatically
resolved — close it as a duplicate.

## Acceptance

- Test `test_move_card_right_selects_moved_card_in_kanban_view` (and a
  matching `test_move_selected_cards_right_selects_first_moved_card`)
  passes.
- The existing `test_create_card_selects_newly_created_card_when_prior_selection_exists`
  (added in KAN-403) stays green.
- `cargo test --workspace`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`,
  `cargo fmt --check` all clean.
- Manual sanity: `kanban /tmp/test.json`, create two cards in Todo,
  select the second, press `l`. Expected: focus follows to Doing
  column, the moved card stays selected.

## Files modified

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs` | Insert `self.prepare_frame()` before `select_card_by_id` at line 480 (`handle_move_card`) and line 536 (`move_selected_cards`). Two lines added. |
| `crates/kanban-tui/tests/stale_model_regression_tests.rs` | Two new tests (single-card and multi-select). |

## Architectural principle

Selection / cursor logic stays inside the TUI app. The fix lives in
`crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs`; nothing leaks into
`kanban-service` or `kanban-domain`. Verified during KAN-403 — no
service- or domain-layer code references `select_card_by_id`,
`active_card`, or `selected_card`.

## Risk

Very low. Same shape as the KAN-403 fix that just merged. The added
`prepare_frame` call is idempotent — the next render-loop call is
redundant but harmless.

## Relationship to other cards

- **KAN-403** (PR #260): identical bug at `create_card`, now fixed.
  This card is the natural follow-up surfaced by KAN-403's plan
  ("`handle_move_card` may have a related bug in ColumnView").
- **Companion refactor card** (filed alongside): centralize the
  refresh in `select_card_by_id`. Implementing the companion subsumes
  this card.

## Commit shape

If going with option 1 (per-handler):

1. `test(tui): cover selector-follows-card on h/l move (red)` — two new
   tests, both failing.
2. `fix(tui): refresh task list before selecting moved card` —
   two-line insertion.
3. `chore: add changeset for KAN-NNN`.

If going with option 2: see companion card.
cards.title: fix(tui): selector does not follow card after move across columns in kanban view

cards.description:
## Why

KAN-403 fixed a TUI regression where `create_card` left the selector on
the prior card instead of jumping to the new one. The root cause was a
stale view-layer task list at the moment `select_card_by_id` ran. The
companion card (filed alongside this one) calls out the same shape at
`handle_move_card` and `move_selected_cards`.

The pattern recurs every time a handler:

1. Mutates the model in a way that adds a card to (or moves a card
   between) view-layer task lists, AND
2. Calls `select_card_by_id(card_id)` before the next render-loop
   `prepare_frame()` has refreshed the task list.

Today the workaround is local to each handler: insert
`self.prepare_frame()` right before `self.select_card_by_id(...)`.
That works but it duplicates the same fix across N handlers, and any
future handler that forgets to add it will silently regress the
selection behaviour.

The cleaner architecture: make `select_card_by_id` itself responsible
for ensuring the active task list is fresh. Selection / cursor logic
already lives entirely inside the TUI app (verified during KAN-403 —
no other crate touches it) and `select_card_by_id` is the canonical
entry point for "the user wants card `X` selected". Refreshing the
task list before searching for `X` is part of that contract.

## What

Modify `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs:1454::select_card_by_id` to
call `self.prepare_frame()` before delegating to
`task_list.select_card(card_id)`:

```rust
pub fn select_card_by_id(&mut self, card_id: uuid::Uuid) {
    self.prepare_frame();
    if let Some(task_list) = self.view.strategy.get_active_task_list_mut() {
        task_list.select_card(card_id);
    }
}
```

Then remove the now-redundant `self.prepare_frame()` calls from:

- `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs::create_card` (the
  one inserted by KAN-403 at line 390).
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs::handle_move_card`
  and `move_selected_cards` (only if the companion card landed first
  and added them — otherwise they were never inserted).

## Where the wiring lives

- `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs:1454` — single method change. ~3
  lines added (the `prepare_frame()` call + a brief doc comment if
  warranted).
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs:390` — remove the
  redundant call inserted by KAN-403.
- Any other handler that's accumulated the same workaround over time.

The `prepare_frame()` method (`app/mod.rs:1541`) and the
`select_card_by_id` method (`app/mod.rs:1454`) both live in
`impl App` in the `kanban-tui` crate. Service and domain layers are
untouched. The selection-logic-stays-in-TUI principle is preserved.

## Existing call sites

`select_card_by_id` is called from 8 handler sites in
`crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/`:

- `card_handlers.rs:252` (`toggle_card_completion`)
- `card_handlers.rs:297` (`toggle_selected_cards_completion`)
- `card_handlers.rs:391` (`create_card` — KAN-403's fix is the
  `prepare_frame` above this line)
- `card_handlers.rs:480` (`handle_move_card`)
- `card_handlers.rs:536` (`move_selected_cards`)
- `card_handlers.rs:603`/`612` (next/prev navigation — card is
  already in the list)
- `column_handlers.rs:537`

For the navigation cases (next/prev, line 603/612), the card is
already in the active task list — calling `prepare_frame` is wasted
work but doesn't change behaviour. For everything else, the refresh
either is the bug fix (create/move) or is a no-op (toggle).

## Performance consideration

Every `select_card_by_id` call now triggers a snapshot load + a
task-list rebuild. For the in-memory backend this is microseconds.
For SQLite it's a single indexed query. Negligible at user-driven
cadence. If a future hot path emerges that calls `select_card_by_id`
in a tight loop, that loop should batch via direct task-list access
anyway, not through this entry point.

## Acceptance

- The redundant `prepare_frame` call removed from `create_card` —
  `test_create_card_selects_newly_created_card_when_prior_selection_exists`
  still passes.
- If the companion card's tests exist:
  `test_move_card_right_selects_moved_card_in_kanban_view` and the
  multi-select variant still pass with the per-handler fixes removed.
- No new tests required — both bug-fix cards already pin down the
  behaviour. A negative test (e.g.
  `test_toggle_completion_does_not_drop_selection`) is optional belt-
  and-braces.
- `cargo test --workspace`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`,
  `cargo fmt --check` all clean.

## Files modified

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs` | `select_card_by_id` calls `self.prepare_frame()` first. |
| `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs` | Remove the redundant `self.prepare_frame()` call from `create_card` (and from any other handler that accumulated the same workaround pre-merge). |

## Risk

Low.

- The change is purely additive at the entry point and removes
  duplication at the call sites — no behaviour change for working
  flows.
- Calls `prepare_frame` once on every selection update across all 8
  call sites. For toggle / navigation that's wasted work, but cheap.
- If a future bug surfaces where `select_card_by_id` is called from a
  context where `prepare_frame` would be wrong (e.g. mid-frame, with
  a partially-applied state), revisit with a `select_card_by_id_raw`
  escape hatch.

## Relationship to other cards

- **KAN-403** (merged in #260): introduced the first per-handler
  workaround in `create_card`. The redundant call goes away when this
  card lands.
- **Companion bug card** (filed alongside): adds per-handler
  workarounds at `handle_move_card` and `move_selected_cards`. Either
  card landing alone is fine; if both land, the order is:
  1. Companion lands first → this card removes its workaround too.
  2. This card lands first → companion is auto-resolved, close as dup.

## Commit shape

1. `refactor(tui): select_card_by_id refreshes task list before
   selecting` — change `select_card_by_id` body, remove the redundant
   `prepare_frame` call(s) from handlers.
2. `chore: add changeset for KAN-NNN` — user-facing description noting
   that the underlying flakiness is gone (most users won't notice
   anything since KAN-403 + the companion bug card already covered
   the visible cases).

## Out of scope

- A "deferred selection intent" mechanism (storing
  `pending_card_selection: Option<Uuid>` on the app and applying it
  during the next `update_cards`) is an alternative that avoids the
  extra `prepare_frame` call but adds plumbing across two layers. Not
  worth the complexity unless `prepare_frame` cost becomes measurable.
cards.title: refactor(tui): centralize task-list refresh in select_card_by_id

cards.description:
Follow-up from PR #247 (Amanda Frost's Windows duplicate-keypress fix).

Amanda noted that the 'Areas for Contribution' section of CONTRIBUTING.md does not explicitly mention that bug fixes are accepted, which made her hesitant to open the PR. Update the section to state plainly that fixes — especially platform-portability fixes — are welcome.

Scope:
- Edit the 'Areas for Contribution' section of CONTRIBUTING.md to call out bug fixes as a welcomed contribution category alongside features.
- Consider adding a brief note that cross-platform / portability fixes are particularly valued.
cards.title: Update CONTRIBUTING.md to explicitly welcome bug fixes

cards.description:
Surfaced by Amanda Frost while working on PR #247.

Two tests in crates/kanban-service/src/config.rs fail on Windows because their assertions hardcode forward-slash path separators while Windows produces backslashes:

```
---- config::tests::test_dto_from_config_with_data_file_shows_storage_fields stdout ----
thread 'config::tests::test_dto_from_config_with_data_file_shows_storage_fields' panicked at crates\kanban-service\src\config.rs:687:9:
got: Some("...coding\\kanban\\crates\\kanban-service\\kanban.json")

---- config::tests::test_dto_from_explicit_config_preserves_values stdout ----
thread 'config::tests::test_dto_from_explicit_config_preserves_values' panicked at crates\kanban-service\src\config.rs:712:9:
got: Some("...\\coding\\kanban\\crates\\kanban-service\\kanban.sqlite")
```

Scope:
- Locate the path-equality assertions at config.rs:687 and config.rs:712.
- Either normalise paths through std::path::PathBuf comparison, or compare against an OS-aware expected value built with PathBuf::from / Path::join, rather than a literal string with forward slashes.
- Verify the tests pass on both Linux and Windows.
- Sweep the rest of crates/kanban-service for similar string-based path assertions that may have the same problem.
cards.title: Fix Windows path-separator assumptions in kanban-service::config tests

cards.description:
## Why this exists

CLAUDE.md (Testing section) prefers `-> KanbanResult<()>` over `#[should_panic]` and notes it composes with `?` for better failure messages. However, a sweep of `crates/kanban-domain` shows **zero** tests use this pattern today — all ~20 tests in `sprint_commands.rs` (and similar counts in `board_commands.rs`, `card_commands.rs`, `column_commands.rs`, `dependency_commands.rs`) use `fn test_*()` with `.unwrap()` chains.

Raised during review of PR #262 (KAN-431). Deferred so the next developer makes the judgment call on full evidence.

## Proposed implementation

Convert tests to the form:

```rust
#[test]
fn test_x() -> KanbanResult<()> {
    let tc = TestContext::new();
    // ...
    let context = tc.as_command_context();
    cmd.execute(&context)?;
    // assertions
    Ok(())
}
```

Replace `.unwrap()` on `KanbanResult`-returning calls with `?`. Tests asserting on errors keep `.unwrap_err()` (no change there).

## Migration path

1. Pick one commands file (`sprint_commands.rs` is a reasonable pilot — well-covered, recently touched).
2. Convert all its tests in one commit: `test(domain): adopt KanbanResult<()> in sprint_commands tests`.
3. Review: does the diff actually read better, or does the noise of `?` everywhere hurt? Are there tests where `.unwrap()` was genuinely clearer?
4. If positive: open follow-ups to convert the other commands files (one commit each).
5. If negative: amend CLAUDE.md to reflect actual project preference for `.unwrap()` in unit tests.

## Decision points

- Worth doing at all? CLAUDE.md is aspirational; the codebase has settled on `.unwrap()`. Either is internally consistent.
- If yes: pilot one file first, evaluate before fanning out.
- Consider whether integration tests in `crates/kanban-service/tests/`, `crates/kanban-tui/tests/`, etc. should also migrate or stay as-is.
cards.title: Decide on KanbanResult<()> test return-type pattern

cards.description:
## Why this exists

Raised during review of PR #262 (KAN-431). The PR extracted three helpers out of `UpdateSprint::execute` in `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/sprint_commands.rs`:

- `validate_card_prefix_not_locked` (sprint_commands.rs:76-92) — reads `list_cards_by_sprint` and `list_archived_cards` via `context.store`.
- `validate_card_prefix_unique` (sprint_commands.rs:94-133) — reads `list_sprints_by_board` via `context.store`.
- `allocate_sprint_name` (sprint_commands.rs:135-147) — reads sprint + board, mutates board, **writes** via `context.store.upsert_board`.

All three are technically domain-legal: `CommandContext.store: &'a dyn DataStore` is a domain-owned port (`kanban-domain::data_store`), not an infrastructure type. But semantically they are orchestration (fetch → check or mutate → persist), exactly what the PR description says is being lifted out of the domain layer.

## Proposed direction

The naming concern surfaced in review (e.g. "`allocate_sprint_name` hides an `upsert_board` side effect") is a symptom of misplaced layering, not a naming problem. Solving it with a rename would paint over the issue.

Two paths to evaluate:

### 1. Fold into KAN-427 / KAN-428 / KAN-429 / KAN-430

These remaining four cards in the same initiative are explicitly about moving orchestration to `kanban-service`. The three helpers in question are natural candidates to move when that work lands. No standalone work needed; just make sure the scoping of the remaining cards explicitly covers them.

### 2. Standalone follow-up

If the remaining cards focus on other command surfaces (not `UpdateSprint`), open a small PR that:

- Moves `validate_card_prefix_not_locked`, `validate_card_prefix_unique`, and the persistence half of `allocate_sprint_name` into `kanban-service`.
- Keeps the pure mutation (`Board::add_sprint_name_at_used_index`, already on `Board`) in domain.
- Reduces `UpdateSprint::execute` to a pure model-update; the service composes validation + execution + persistence.

## Decision points

- Read the scope of KAN-427, KAN-428, KAN-429, KAN-430 first. If `UpdateSprint` is in any of their scopes, fold this in there.
- If not, decide whether a small standalone PR is worth it now or whether to leave the helpers in domain until a more comprehensive layering pass.
- Either way, the rename suggestion from PR #262's review is dropped — relocating supersedes it.
cards.title: Review domain-vs-service layering of UpdateSprint helpers

cards.description:
After the boards.{json,sqlite} default-filename rename (PR #249, branch
KAN-332/require-explicit-file-path-no-implicit-kanban-json-default), three
groups of stale references were intentionally left in place. This card covers
sweeping them so the repo is fully consistent.

## Scope

### D. Test-internal temp filenames (mostly safe to rename, but pure churn)

Most of these are arbitrary `TempDir::new().path().join("…")` paths — the
filename has no semantic meaning, only the test's setup needs an identifier.
They don't break anything today; renaming is a hygiene/consistency pass.

Files (rename `kanban.json` → `boards.json`, `kanban.sqlite` → `boards.sqlite`,
`myboard.*` → `boards.*` unless the test specifically exercises a non-default
custom name):

- `crates/kanban-cli/tests/migrate.rs:250-251, 289-290`
  (`myboard.json` / `myboard.db` / `myboard.sqlite` — used as source/dest
   paths in migrate integration tests)
- `crates/kanban-cli/tests/cli_integration.rs:2333-2334`
  (asserts no `kanban.json` is auto-created — keep the assertion semantics
   but pick a name that matches today's docs; consider `boards.json`)
- `crates/kanban-cli/tests/cli_app_run_no_backends.rs:21`
  (constructs a json_path inside a TempDir)
- `crates/kanban-tui/tests/backend_selection_tests.rs:77-78`
  (cwd-guarded \"no `kanban.json` in cwd\" assertion — see KAN-332)
- `crates/kanban-tui/tests/import_failure_safety.rs:9, 65, 82`
  (three TempDir + join paths)
- `crates/kanban-tui/tests/conflict_detection_tests.rs:12, 69, 80, 115, 174,
   218` (five file_path locals + one substring assertion at :69 that
   checks the path contains \"kanban.json\")
- `crates/kanban-tui/tests/settings_config_edit_tests.rs:173, 200, 256`
  (comments referencing the old default — update to mention `boards.json`)
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs:2410`
  (manually-constructed `App` for the `test_save_worker_does_not_send_completion_on_conflict`
   test — `config_storage_location: \"kanban.json\".into()` placeholder)

Plan:
1. `sed -i 's|\"kanban\\.json\"|\"boards.json\"|g; s|\"kanban\\.sqlite\"|\"boards.sqlite\"|g; s|\"myboard\\.json\"|\"boards.json\"|g; s|\"myboard\\.sqlite\"|\"boards.sqlite\"|g; s|\"myboard\\.db\"|\"boards.db\"|g'` over the test files listed above.
2. Update the substring assertion in `conflict_detection_tests.rs:69` to match
   the new filename.
3. Update the prose comments in `settings_config_edit_tests.rs:173, 200, 256`.
4. Run `cargo test --workspace` to confirm nothing relied on the old name.
5. One commit: `chore: rename test temp filenames to boards.{json,sqlite}`.

### E. Historical changeset wording (intentionally NOT renaming)

Two changesets describe behaviour from before the boards.{json,sqlite} rename,
and renaming inside them would distort the historical record:

- `.changeset/kan-332-require-explicit-file-path-no-implicit-kanban-json-default.md`
  — describes removing the implicit `./kanban.json` fallback. The literal
   file name is the *historical* default the user is being warned about.
- `.changeset/kan-405-json-backend-should-not-persist-command-log-between-sessions.md:7`
  — \"the command log is never written to `kanban.json` or SQLite\". This
   sentence describes legacy persistence behaviour at a point in time when
   the default was still `kanban.json`.

Resolution: leave both alone. Add a single line at the top of each
referencing this card so future readers know the wording is deliberate.
Or, alternatively, rephrase to not mention the literal filename at all
(\"the implicit default JSON file\" / \"the JSON file or SQLite\").

Plan:
1. Decide between (a) leave + add cross-reference, or (b) genericise the
   wording. Default: (b), genericise — the changeset's job is to describe
   the *change* in behaviour, not to pin a filename in time.
2. One commit: `chore: genericise changeset wording for filename rename`.

## Acceptance criteria

- `grep -rn 'kanban\\.json\\|kanban\\.sqlite\\|myboard\\.' --include='*.rs' --include='*.md'` returns 0 hits across the repo (excluding the deliberate historical changeset references if (a) is chosen).
- `cargo test --workspace`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo fmt --all -- --check` all pass.
- A single PR per group (D and E in separate PRs, or one combined) targeting `develop`.

## Why this was deferred

PR #249's primary scope was KAN-332 (gate subcommands behind explicit file)
and KAN-330 (startup dialog). The default-filename rename to boards.{json,sqlite}
landed in #249 because it directly touched user-visible defaults. The
test-internal renames and historical changeset wording are pure churn /
historical accuracy questions and would have widened the diff without
adding value to that PR's review.
cards.title: Sweep stale 'kanban.json' / 'myboard.*' references in tests and historical docs
Decouple kanban-persistence from kanban-domain

cards.description:
## Principle

Storage backends must never be chosen by filename extension. Extensions are
a user-facing convenience for at-a-glance identification, not a dispatch
mechanism. The authoritative source of truth is:

1. **Existing files**: magic-byte / content sniffing (each backend's
   `StoreFactory::matches_content`).
2. **New files**: the user's explicit choice — CLI `--backend` flag, the
   choose-storage dialog radio, or config's `storage_backend`.
3. **Filename extension**: cosmetic only. A user picking SQLite in the
   dialog and typing `boards.json` should still get a SQLite backend; the
   extension is just a label.

Each storage backend crate (`kanban-persistence-json`,
`kanban-persistence-sqlite`) should expose a public `EXTENSIONS: &[&str]`
constant (or `StoreFactory::extensions() -> &'static [&'static str]`
trait method) so consumers stop hardcoding `.json` / `.sqlite` /
`.sqlite3` / `.db` strings throughout the codebase.

## Inventory of hardcoded extension knowledge today

### Production dispatch / state

- **`crates/kanban-service/src/store_manager.rs:47-57`** — `is_sqlite()`
  falls through to `locator.ends_with(".sqlite")` / `.sqlite3` / `.db`.
  This is the **primary violation**: `make_backend` calls it before
  doing content detection, so a non-existent file with a `.sqlite`
  extension gets a SQLite backend regardless of explicit selection.
  Authoritative SQLite-extension list lives here today.

- **`crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs:120-129`** —
  `StorageBackendChoice::extension()` returns `.json` / `.sqlite`. Used
  by the dialog Tab toggle to swap the filename's extension. After this
  refactor: the radio still toggles the displayed filename for UX, but
  the backend choice is read from `app.choose_storage_backend`, not
  inferred from the path.

- **`crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs:138-149`** —
  `swap_known_extension`'s `KNOWN: &[&str] = &[".json", ".sqlite",
  ".sqlite3", ".db"]`. Duplicates the list from `store_manager`. Should
  source from each backend crate's `EXTENSIONS` constant.

- **`crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/board_handlers.rs:146-162`** —
  `scan_import_files()` filters the import dialog by `.json` only.
  Probably intentional because JSON is the export format, but
  `ExportFormat::Sqlite` exists too — SQLite exports can't be re-imported
  through this dialog. Either widen to all backends' `EXTENSIONS` or
  document the JSON-only constraint.

### Backend factories (already correct — content-based)

- `crates/kanban-persistence-json/src/lib.rs:19-28` —
  `JsonStoreFactory::matches_content` checks for `{` / `[` after BOM and
  whitespace. **Content-based, no extension involved.** Model to follow.
- `crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/lib.rs:15-17` —
  `SqliteStoreFactory::matches_content` checks for the
  `b\"SQLite format 3\\0\"` magic bytes. **Same.**

### Test guardrails (lower priority — keep but consolidate)

`.ends_with(\".json\")` assertions in:
- `crates/kanban-cli/tests/cli_app_builder.rs:34, 46, 63`
- `crates/kanban-mcp/tests/mcp_server_builder.rs:35, 47`
- `crates/kanban-service/tests/store_manager_injection.rs:89-90`
- `crates/kanban-service/tests/make_store.rs:16, 70`

These all assert the **system default backend** is JSON. They're correct
guardrails — if the default flips they fail loudly. After exposing
`EXTENSIONS`, they should iterate the constant rather than inline
`\".json\"`.

## Proposed architecture

### 1. Per-crate `EXTENSIONS` exports

```rust
// crates/kanban-persistence-json/src/lib.rs
pub const EXTENSIONS: &[&str] = &[\".json\"];

// crates/kanban-persistence-sqlite/src/lib.rs
pub const EXTENSIONS: &[&str] = &[\".sqlite\", \".sqlite3\", \".db\"];
```

Optionally extend the `StoreFactory` trait so consumers can iterate
without depending on each crate by name:

```rust
// crates/kanban-persistence/src/registry.rs
pub trait StoreFactory: Send + Sync {
    // ... existing methods ...

    /// File extensions (with leading dot) commonly used for this
    /// backend. Cosmetic — extensions never drive backend dispatch.
    fn extensions(&self) -> &'static [&'static str] { &[] }
}
```

### 2. `make_backend` signature change

From:
```rust
pub async fn make_backend(&self, locator: &str, config: &AppConfig)
    -> Result<Arc<dyn KanbanBackend>, KanbanError>
```

To something like:
```rust
pub async fn make_backend(
    &self,
    locator: &str,
    explicit_backend: Option<&str>,  // CLI flag, dialog radio, config
    config: &AppConfig,
) -> Result<Arc<dyn KanbanBackend>, KanbanError>
```

Resolution order:
1. **File exists**: try each factory's `matches_content` on the first N
   bytes; first match wins. Conflict between content and explicit
   backend → log and trust content.
2. **File does not exist**: use `explicit_backend` (or fall back to
   `config.effective_storage_backend()`).
3. **Filename extension**: ignored.

### 3. Drop / rewrite `is_sqlite(locator)`

The current implementation is the single biggest violation of the
principle. Either remove it entirely or keep only the content-detection
path (the `Some(\"sqlite\") => true` branch via `detect_backend`).

### 4. Dialog state plumbed through to `make_backend`

`adopt_storage_file` already knows `app.choose_storage_backend`. Pass
that to `make_backend` as `explicit_backend`. Tab in the dialog
continues to swap the visible filename extension for UX, but the
backend itself is determined by the radio.

### 5. Import dialog widens (optional)

`scan_import_files` should iterate
`json_factory.extensions()` ∪ `sqlite_factory.extensions()`
(or call into a helper on `StoreManager`) instead of hardcoding
`.json`.

## Open design questions

1. **`extensions()` as a trait method vs. crate-level `pub const`?** Trait
   method is more polymorphic (consumers iterate via the registry and
   never reach into specific crates); const is zero-cost and more
   discoverable in IDE. Probably trait method with a default `&[]` so
   third-party factories don't have to implement it.

2. **What if explicit backend conflicts with content detection?** E.g.
   user passes `kanban --backend sqlite my-existing-json-file.json`.
   - Trust content (current behaviour for the SQLite-magic case): warn
     loudly, use JSON.
   - Trust user: refuse to open and error out.
   - Default proposal: trust content + warn. Same shape as the existing
     `sync_backend_with_file` flow.

3. **For commands that genuinely do not have a file yet** (e.g., bare
   TUI launch, `kanban migrate <src> <target_backend>`): the explicit
   choice is the only signal. No change for these.

4. **Backwards compatibility**: changing `make_backend`'s signature is a
   breaking change to `kanban-service`'s public API. Ripples to TUI /
   CLI / MCP call sites. Worth a single PR that touches all three; not
   suitable as a partial migration.

## Acceptance criteria

- `kanban-persistence-json` and `kanban-persistence-sqlite` each expose
  their `EXTENSIONS` constant (and/or implement `StoreFactory::extensions`).
- `StoreManager::is_sqlite(locator)` removed, or rewritten to use only
  content sniffing (no `ends_with` calls).
- `StoreManager::make_backend` accepts an explicit backend choice and
  prefers it over filename inference.
- `swap_known_extension` in `kanban-tui` sources its known list from the
  persistence crates' `EXTENSIONS` (via `StoreManager` or directly).
- `StorageBackendChoice::extension()` returns the *first* (canonical)
  entry from each backend's `EXTENSIONS` rather than a hardcoded literal.
- `scan_import_files` either iterates all backends' extensions or has a
  comment justifying the JSON-only filter.
- The `.ends_with(\".json\")` test assertions cited above either iterate
  `JSON_EXTENSIONS` or are rewritten to assert on backend identity
  rather than path suffix.
- `cargo test --workspace`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets`,
  `cargo fmt --all -- --check` all clean.

## Why this was deferred from PR #249

PR #249's scope was KAN-332 (gate subcommands behind explicit file) and
KAN-330 (startup dialog). Adding the radio in #249 *exposed* the latent
extension-vs-explicit-choice tension, but fixing it requires changing
`StoreManager::make_backend`'s public signature, which ripples through
TUI / CLI / MCP. A targeted PR for this refactor lets reviewers focus on
the dispatch model without being mixed up with dialog-UX changes.
cards.title: Backend dispatch must be content-driven, not filename-driven; expose extensions per backend crate

cards.description: parse_editor() calls shell_words::split().unwrap_or_default(), which silently swallows an Err when EDITOR contains unclosed quotes (e.g. EDITOR='vim). The result is an empty program string, which propagates through resolve_editor and eventually fails with a cryptic OS error from Command::new("") rather than a clear message to the user. Fix: return a Result from parse_editor, propagate the error to edit_in_external_editor with an io::Error::new(io::ErrorKind::InvalidInput, ...) and an actionable message like 'Could not parse EDITOR value: unclosed quote'.
cards.title: editor: handle malformed EDITOR value (unclosed quotes)

cards.description: shell_words uses POSIX quoting rules where backslash is an escape character. A Windows user who sets EDITOR=C:\Users\foo\vim.exe (no quotes, backslashes) will have their path silently mangled — the backslashes are interpreted as escapes. The quoted-path tests in the PR use forward slashes only. Options: (1) document in README that Windows users must use forward slashes or quote the path; (2) gate on cfg!(target_os='windows') and use a simpler whitespace split when unquoted. At minimum, add a test case with a raw backslash Windows path to make the failure visible.
cards.title: editor: document/handle Windows backslash paths in EDITOR

cards.description: resolve_editor_uses_env_var and resolve_editor_handles_multiword_editor both check that the returned path ends_with('vim') or ends_with('code'), but which::which falls back to PathBuf::from(program) on failure — meaning PathBuf::from('vim').ends_with('vim') is true even when vim is not on PATH. The tests pass vacuously on machines without the editors installed. Fix: assert that the path is absolute (path.is_absolute()) as a proxy for 'which actually resolved it', or split into two cases: when the binary is present assert absolute, when absent assert the program name passthrough.
cards.title: editor: strengthen resolve_editor test assertions
